runbook_bb120c6e.name: TCAM Resource Analysis
runbook_bb120c6e.description: Analyze TCAM utilization and predict ACL resource exhaustion. Uses context-aware thresholds: control-plane TCAM at 50-60% is normal (static punt/trap entries); only flags at 85%+ warning / 95%+ critical. Use when ACLs are failing to install or TCAM is running out.

cli_commands.command: show platform hardware fed switch active fwd-asic resource tcam utilization
cli_commands.purpose: Check TCAM resource utilization across all regions

interpretation_rules.message: ACL/QoS TCAM region is at or above 75% — monitor for capacity issues
interpretation_rules.recommendation: Review ACL/QoS entries for optimisation; consider object-groups or SDM template reallocation

interpretation_rules.message: ACL/QoS TCAM region is at or above 90% — feature programming may fail
interpretation_rules.recommendation: Immediate action needed: consolidate ACL entries, remove unused ACLs, or reallocate SDM template

interpretation_rules.message: Forwarding TCAM region is at or above 80% — monitor for growth
interpretation_rules.recommendation: Baseline and trend this value; plan SDM template changes if additional routes/MACs expected

interpretation_rules.message: Forwarding TCAM region is at or above 95% — routes/MACs may not be programmed in hardware
interpretation_rules.recommendation: Immediate action: check for route/MAC leaks, summarise routes, or adjust SDM template

interpretation_rules.message: Control-plane TCAM region is at or above 85% — elevated but not yet critical. Note: 50-60% is normal baseline due to static punt/trap entries (ARP, DHCP, STP, routing, CoPP).
interpretation_rules.recommendation: Identify any unexpected control-plane entries; trend over time to detect growth

interpretation_rules.message: Control-plane TCAM region is at or above 95% — punt entries may fail to install
interpretation_rules.recommendation: Immediate investigation required: check for unexpected CoPP entries or feature overlap

cli_commands.command: show platform software fed switch active acl resource usage
cli_commands.purpose: View ACL resource usage
Display configured access lists
Review ACL configurations

analysis_points: Control-plane TCAM at 50-60% is NORMAL (static punt/trap entries) — only flag at 85%+
ACL/QoS regions are the most constrained — flag at 75%+ warning, 90%+ critical
Forwarding (MAC/Router/Multicast) regions — flag at 80%+ warning, 95%+ critical
Check SDM template allocation if any region approaches limits
Look for TCAM exhaustion syslogs (%FED-1-TCAM_RESOURCE) in device logs

runbook_d1e6ed2d.name: ICMP For-Us Attack Detection with Hardware Analysis
runbook_d1e6ed2d.description: Comprehensive workflow to detect ICMP flood attacks targeting switch management IP (for-us traffic). Analyzes hardware CoPP protection, punt statistics, CPU queues, and bandwidth impact using platform-level diagnostics. Includes per-command interpretation rules for automated verdict generation.

cli_commands.purpose: Identify CPU utilization and process-level impact from ICMP traffic

interpretation_rules.message: CPU utilization exceeds 50% — switch control plane is under stress
interpretation_rules.recommendation: Immediately check for punt storm; apply CoPP rate limiting

interpretation_rules.message: CPU utilization above 5% — may indicate punt traffic reaching CPU
interpretation_rules.recommendation: Monitor trend; check punt cause summary for elevated counters

interpretation_rules.message: Sustained CPU above 20% over 1 minute — significant control plane impact
interpretation_rules.recommendation: Engage TAC; check for punt storm or process-level CPU hog

cli_commands.purpose: Get comprehensive ICMP statistics including echo requests/replies counts

interpretation_rules.message: ICMP echo request count exceeds 100K — ICMP flood attack confirmed
interpretation_rules.recommendation: Apply ingress ACL to block/rate-limit ICMP echo; identify source IP with packet trace

interpretation_rules.message: ICMP echo request count above 10K — elevated ICMP traffic, monitor trend
interpretation_rules.recommendation: Take a second sample after 60 seconds to calculate pps rate

interpretation_rules.message: Switch is replying to >100K ICMP requests — acting as reflector
interpretation_rules.recommendation: Disable ICMP echo reply or apply CoPP to limit response rate

interpretation_rules.message: High ICMP unreachable generation — possible scanning or routing black-hole
interpretation_rules.recommendation: Check for route churn or network scanning activity

cli_commands.command: show platform software fed switch active punt cause summary
cli_commands.purpose: Analyze punt path statistics to identify for-us traffic patterns

interpretation_rules.message: For-us data punt count exceeds 1M — massive for-us traffic hitting CPU
interpretation_rules.recommendation: Correlate with ICMP echo count; apply CoPP or ingress ACL

interpretation_rules.message: Punt path is dropping for-us packets — punt queue congestion detected
interpretation_rules.recommendation: CoPP is actively rate-limiting; verify no legitimate traffic is being dropped

cli_commands.command: show platform software fed switch active punt cpuq all
cli_commands.purpose: Check CPU queue statistics for ICMP GEN and for-us traffic queues

interpretation_rules.message: For-us traffic CPU queue has RX drops — queue overflow detected
interpretation_rules.recommendation: Punt rate exceeds CPU processing capacity; apply hardware-level filtering

interpretation_rules.message: ICMP GEN queue has >100K packets — ICMP traffic being punted to CPU
interpretation_rules.recommendation: Verify ICMP is not being software-switched; check for missing adjacency

interpretation_rules.message: For-us queue experienced RX suspend events — severe congestion
interpretation_rules.recommendation: CPU is overwhelmed; immediately apply ingress ACL to drop attack traffic

cli_commands.command: show platform hardware fed switch active qos queue stats internal cpu policer
cli_commands.purpose: Verify hardware CoPP policer stats and check for drops

interpretation_rules.message: Hardware CoPP policer is dropping for-us traffic frames — rate limit active
interpretation_rules.recommendation: CoPP is protecting the CPU; verify policer rate is appropriate

interpretation_rules.message: ICMP GEN hardware policer is actively dropping — ICMP rate exceeds policer limit
interpretation_rules.recommendation: Good — CoPP is working; consider lowering the policer rate to be more aggressive

cli_commands.command: show platform software fed switch active inject cause summary
cli_commands.purpose: Check inject statistics to verify hardware ICMP response processing

cli_commands.command: show platform software fed switch active acl counters hardware
cli_commands.purpose: Review hardware ACL counters for traffic patterns
Check interface packet counters and bandwidth utilization
Check packet capture status for ICMP analysis
Verify control plane protection policies are active

interpretation_rules.message: CoPP forus policy is dropping traffic — exceeded byte count > 0
interpretation_rules.recommendation: CoPP is engaged and protecting CPU; review dropped traffic type

interpretation_rules.message: CoPP data policer (ICMP/Broadcast) is dropping traffic
interpretation_rules.recommendation: ICMP rate exceeds the data policer; consider hardening with ACL

analysis_points: CRITICAL: Check show ip traffic ICMP echo request count - baseline <10K, attack >100K
CRITICAL: Verify ICMP echo requests vs replies ratio - should be roughly equal
CRITICAL: Check CPU utilization - should remain low (<5%) if hardware CoPP working
Check CPU Queue 3 (ICMP GEN) packet count in cpuq stats
Check CPU Queue 2 (For-us traffic) packet count in cpuq stats
Verify Cause 11 (For-us data) in punt cause summary statistics
Check hardware policer stats - Policer 0 handles ICMP GEN queue
Verify no drops in hardware CoPP policers - drops indicate rate limiting active
Calculate bandwidth impact: (ICMP count x 128 bytes) for total consumption
Check inject cause summary for L2 control and QFP statistics
Verify interface packet rate matches ICMP traffic volume
CRITICAL: Hardware acceleration - if ICMP count is high but CPU queue low, ASIC is handling
Check for punt path congestion - RX dropped count should be zero
Verify control plane protection is enabled and enforcing rate limits
Identify if ICMP is being hardware-processed (normal) vs CPU-punted (abnormal)
Calculate ICMP rate: new_count - old_count over time interval
Check second-level policer 21 (ios-feature) for ICMP protection stats
Verify no RX suspend events in CPU queues indicating congestion

analysis_points: CRITICAL: Identify source IP address(es) from captured packets
CRITICAL: Identify source MAC address(es) to locate attacking device
CRITICAL: Identify physical ingress interface where attack traffic enters
CRITICAL: Identify logical interface (VLAN) associated with attack traffic
Check if attack is from single source (targeted) or multiple sources (distributed)
Analyze packet timing intervals to identify attack pattern (burst vs steady)
Verify punt cause code - should be 'For-us data' (11) for attacks targeting switch
Check CPU queue number - different queues for different traffic types
Analyze packet size consistency - identical sizes indicate scripted attack
Check TTL values - low TTL indicates distant source, high TTL indicates nearby
Verify destination IP matches switch management IP or SVI
Check destination MAC - should be switch MAC for for-us traffic
Calculate attack rate from capture: packets captured / capture duration
Compare captured packet count vs total in 'show ip traffic' for scale assessment
Identify protocol-specific details (ICMP type, TCP flags, UDP ports)
Check for multiple attack vectors (mixed protocols) from packet types
Verify if packets are being punted (captured) or hardware-processed
Document attacker MAC for MAC-based blocking or port-security
Document attacker IP for ACL-based blocking
Correlate physical interface to network topology to identify attack entry point

runbook_a67ad0be.name: EVPN VXLAN VNI Scale TCAM Validation
runbook_a67ad0be.description: Detects silent VNI-to-VLAN TCAM (Lisp Inst Mapping / vnid_trans) overflow when scaling EVPN VXLAN fabrics. The hardware ASIC has a fixed limit on VNI translation entries (~992 per ASIC on Cat9k). When the software VNI count exceeds this limit, excess VNIs show 'Up' in 'show nve vni' but are NOT programmed in hardware TCAM. Incoming VXLAN traffic for unprogrammed VNIs hits the catchall entry and is silently dropped — no syslog or error is generated. Run 'EVPN VXLAN Doppler Platform Troubleshooting' parent workflow first.
symptoms: VNIs show Up in 'show nve vni' but VXLAN traffic for some VNIs is silently dropped
Some VNIs forward traffic normally while recently added VNIs do not
Scaled EVPN fabric with hundreds of VNIs but subset of VNIs have no data-plane forwarding
No error or syslog when adding VNIs beyond hardware capacity
VXLAN decapsulation fails for specific VNIs while others work on the same VTEP
Lisp Inst Mapping TCAM utilization at or near 100%
show platform software fed vni all shows fewer entries than show nve vni
Hardware forwarding counters show 0 for specific VNIs while software shows them Up

cli_commands.purpose: Get full list of software VNIs with state. Count total L2/L3 VNIs. All VNIs will show 'Up' even if they are NOT programmed in hardware TCAM

cli_commands.command: show platform hardware fed switch active fwd-asic resource tcam utilization
cli_commands.purpose: CRITICAL: Check 'Lisp Inst Mapping' TCAM row. Compare 'Used' vs 'Max' values. If Used equals Max, the TCAM is full and additional VNIs cannot be programmed in hardware. Typical Max is 992 on Cat9300/9400. The catchall entry (1 entry) is always present, so usable capacity = Max - 1

interpretation_rules.message: Lisp Inst Mapping TCAM is at or above 95% — VNIs may be silently unprogrammed in hardware
interpretation_rules.recommendation: Compare software VNI count (show nve vni) vs TCAM Used count. Reduce VNI count to stay within hardware limit, or redistribute VNIs across multiple switches/ASICs

interpretation_rules.field: Lisp Inst Mapping TCAM utilization warning
interpretation_rules.message: Lisp Inst Mapping TCAM is above 85% — approaching hardware VNI limit
interpretation_rules.recommendation: Plan for VNI capacity. Adding more VNIs may silently fail to program in hardware without any error indication

cli_commands.command: show platform software fed switch active vni all
cli_commands.purpose: List all VNIs programmed in the FED software layer with their VLAN mappings. Compare count against show nve vni and TCAM entries to find discrepancies

cli_commands.command: show platform hardware fed switch active fwd-asic resource tcam table vnid_trans format 1 | count TranslatedInstance
cli_commands.purpose: Count the number of VNI-to-VLAN translation entries actually programmed in the hardware ASIC TCAM. Subtract 1 for the catchall entry. If this count is less than the software VNI count from 'show nve vni', some VNIs are NOT in hardware
cli_commands.capture_pattern: Number of lines which match regexp\s*=\s*(\d+)

cli_commands.purpose: Count total VNIs in Up state in software. Compare with hw_tcam_vni_count: if sw_count > hw_count, the difference represents silently unprogrammed VNIs
cli_commands.capture_pattern: Number of lines which match regexp\s*=\s*(\d+)

cli_commands.command: show platform hardware fed switch active fwd-asic resource tcam utilization | include Lisp
cli_commands.purpose: Focused check on Lisp Inst Mapping TCAM row only. Shows Used/Max at a glance to quickly determine if the hardware limit is reached

Check the SDM (Switch Database Management) template in use. Different SDM templates allocate different TCAM sizes to the Lisp Inst Mapping region. Switching to a template with larger VNI allocation may increase capacity

Check for any scale-related syslog messages. NOTE: TCAM overflow for VNI translation is typically SILENT — no syslog is generated, making this issue very hard to detect without proactive TCAM monitoring

analysis_points: CRITICAL: Compare software VNI count ('show nve vni | count Up') against hardware TCAM entries ('show platform hardware fed ... tcam table vnid_trans format 1 | count TranslatedInstance' minus 1 for catchall). If software count > hardware count, the difference is the number of VNIs that are UP in software but NOT programmed in hardware. Traffic for these VNIs is SILENTLY DROPPED

CRITICAL: Check 'Lisp Inst Mapping' row in TCAM utilization output. The 'Max' column shows the hardware limit per ASIC (typically 992 on Cat9300/9400). The last entry (index Max-1) is always the catchall, so usable capacity is Max - 1 = 991. If Used == Max, the table is full and no more VNIs can be programmed regardless of what 'show nve vni' reports

CRITICAL: This failure mode is COMPLETELY SILENT. There is no syslog, no error message, no CLI warning when a VNI fails to program in TCAM due to table overflow. The VNI appears as 'Up' in all software commands. Only hardware-level inspection reveals the problem. This makes it a particularly dangerous scale issue that can go undetected in production

CHECK: To identify WHICH specific VNIs are missing from hardware, parse the full output of 'show platform hardware fed switch active fwd-asic resource tcam table vnid_trans format 1'. Extract all TranslatedInstanceId values (these are the VNIs in hardware). Compare against the VNI list from 'show nve vni'. Any VNI present in software but absent from TCAM is unprogrammed

CHECK: The missing VNIs are not necessarily the 'last added' ones. TCAM programming depends on ASIC hashing and entry ordering. Original VNIs configured before the scale-up can also be evicted or fail to reprogram. Always do a full cross-reference, not just check the newest VNIs

CHECK: Both L2 VNIs and L3 VNIs consume entries in the same Lisp Inst Mapping TCAM. When counting capacity, include L2 VNIs (show nve vni | count L2CP), L3 VNIs (show nve vni | count L3CP), and any transit/decap VNIs. Total must not exceed Max - 1

REMEDIATION: If TCAM is full, options include: (1) Reduce total VNI count to stay within hardware limit, (2) Check if a different SDM template allocates more TCAM to Lisp Inst Mapping, (3) Distribute VNIs across multiple switches if the fabric supports it, (4) Upgrade to a platform with larger TCAM capacity. After reducing VNI count, toggle 'shut/no shut' on interface nve1 to force reprogramming of all VNIs in hardware

VERIFY FIX: After remediation, re-run 'show platform hardware fed ... tcam table vnid_trans format 1 | count TranslatedInstance' and confirm the count matches 'show nve vni | count Up' plus 1 (for catchall). Also verify 'show platform hardware fed ... tcam utilization' shows Lisp Inst Mapping Used < Max

runbook_a5469c3b.name: EVPN VXLAN Subnet Slicing Troubleshooting
runbook_a5469c3b.description: Troubleshooting workflow for EVPN Scalable Fabric with Subnet Slicing (aka mini-subnets). Subnet slicing divides a stretched /24 subnet into smaller mini-subnets anchored on border nodes to improve scale: borders hold all hosts in their mini-subnet while leaf switches only hold local host routes and mini-subnet prefixes. Covers subnet carving config, route advertisements, host learning/discovery, selective flooding, traffic flows (E-W, N-S), host mobility, ARP behaviour with multiple subnets, CEF entry behaviour, and microloop prevention. Run 'EVPN VXLAN Doppler Platform Troubleshooting' parent workflow first.
symptoms: Subnet slicing not working — mini-subnet prefixes not appearing on leaf
Host route missing after mini-subnet prefixes are installed
CEF removes Adj-sourced host /32 when mini-subnet /25 arrives
Forwarding loop between leaf and border with decrementing TTL
Microloop detected — same packet seen hundreds of times on fabric uplink
DENY_ROUTE counter stays 0 despite microloop-check Y in CEF
ARP failure for hosts in sliced subnet
Border not using correct mini-subnet gateway for host discovery
Mini-subnet prefix not advertised from anchor/border
Anchor redundancy not working for subnet slice

cli_commands.purpose: Verify subnet slicing config on SVIs. Look for 'evpn subnet-slicing size <N> index <I>' under VLAN interfaces. On anchors (borders): size = number of mini-subnets, index = bucket assigned to this border. On leafs: no subnet-slicing config expected

cli_commands.command: show ip interface brief | include Vlan
cli_commands.purpose: Verify all VLAN SVIs have valid IP addresses. Anchors should have secondary addresses for each mini-subnet gateway (e.g., .126/25 secondary + .254/25 primary for /24 sliced into /25s)

On leaf: verify mini-subnet prefixes (e.g., 192.168.20.0/25, 192.168.20.128/25) appear as BGP routes via borders through L3VNI (Vlan3001). The original /24 connected route should also be present. On anchor: verify mini-subnets are connected/local

Verify CEF entry for sliced prefix. Key flags to check: 'net-slc' in epoch flags = subnet-slice prefix. FIB-FWD-Policy subblock must show 'subnet-slice Y, microloop-check Y'. The microloop-check flag prevents forwarding loops when host /32 is missing from leaf CEF

interpretation_rules.message: CEF prefix is correctly marked as a subnet-slice entry.
Microloop-check is enabled on the sliced prefix. Traffic from fabric matching this prefix without a /32 host route will be denied by hardware (when fix is active).
Microloop-check is NOT set on the sliced prefix. Forwarding loops can occur if host /32 route is missing on leaf.

cli_commands.purpose: Verify local host /32 route in CEF. Before mini-subnets arrive, host is Adj-sourced under the /24 connected prefix. After mini-subnets, CEF removes Adj-sourced /32 if a more-specific subnet covers it — EVPN must install the host route into RIB to maintain the /32. Check 'FIB-FWD-Policy: subnet-slice N, AdjFIB-repl Y' for host entries

cli_commands.command: show platform software fed active ip route vrf {vrf} {mini_subnet_prefix}
cli_commands.purpose: Verify sliced prefix is programmed in FED hardware. Check for 'Forwarding-Policy: microloop-check Y' in the output. The ECMP paths should point to border/anchor VTEPs via L3VNI (Vlan3001)

interpretation_rules.message: FED has microloop-check programmed for sliced prefix.

cli_commands.command: show platform hardware fed active fwd-asic drops exceptions | include DENY
cli_commands.purpose: Check DENY_ROUTE hardware counter. When microloop-check is active in the ASIC, packets hitting a sliced prefix from fabric (without a /32 host route) increment DENY_ROUTE instead of being forwarded. Counter = 0 after reproducing a loop means the hardware fix is NOT active in this image. Non-zero = fix is working

interpretation_rules.message: DENY_ROUTE counter is 0. If a microloop was reproduced, this means the hardware is NOT enforcing the microloop-check deny. The AAL layer fix may be missing from this image.

cli_commands.purpose: Check ARP entries. Borders use the corresponding mini-subnet gateway address for host discovery (e.g., .126 for /25 index 0, .254 for /25 index 1). Hosts on leaf switches can still be learned even though the leaf has no mini-subnet config

Verify SISF device-tracking entries on the leaf. Both hosts within different mini-subnet ranges should be discoverable on the leaf (e.g., .2 in /25[0] and .200 in /25[1]). Borders use corresponding gateway IPs for each mini-subnet range

Verify mini-subnet prefix advertisements from anchors. Each border should advertise its assigned mini-subnet prefix(es) as RT-5. Redundancy groups: multiple borders with same index provide anchor redundancy for that mini-subnet slice

cli_commands.command: monitor capture {capture_name} interface {fabric_uplink} both match any
cli_commands.purpose: MICROLOOP REPRODUCTION: Configure packet capture on the fabric uplink interface (e.g., TwentyFiveGigE1/0/1) of the leaf VTEP being tested. This capture will show looped packets with decrementing TTL values if a microloop exists
MICROLOOP REPRODUCTION: Start the capture before triggering traffic. Send a single ICMP ping to the target host from a remote source while the host /32 route is missing from the leaf
MICROLOOP REPRODUCTION: Stop the capture after the ping completes

MICROLOOP REPRODUCTION: Analyze captured packets. Look for the same ICMP Echo (same id and seq) appearing multiple times with decrementing TTL values (e.g., TTL=252, 251, 250 ... 2, 1). If seen, microloop is confirmed: packet bounces between leaf and border until TTL expires. 1 ping generating 252 copies = loop

interpretation_rules.message: Multiple ICMP packets with same id/seq and decrementing TTL confirms a forwarding loop between leaf and border.

cli_commands.command: test cef route ipv4 vrf {vrf} {host_ip}/32 remove
cli_commands.purpose: MICROLOOP REPRODUCTION (TEST ONLY): Simulate host /32 route loss by removing it from CEF. After removal, traffic to this host will match the /25 sliced prefix and be sent to border, which forwards back to leaf — creating the loop. Use only in lab environments

analysis_points: SUBNET CARVING: Subnet slicing extends the subnet mask to create mini-subnets. A /24 with 'evpn subnet-slicing size 2 index 0' creates two /25s. The 'size' parameter sets the number of extended bits (2 = 2 bits = 4 mini-subnets for /26, or 1 bit = 2 mini-subnets for /25). The 'index' selects which bucket this anchor owns

ANCHOR CONFIG: On borders (anchors), the SVI must have both the original subnet and secondary addresses for mini-subnet gateways. Example for /24->2x/25: primary 192.168.20.254/25, secondary 192.168.20.126/25. Enable 'ip local-proxy-arp' and 'no autostate' on the SVI

LEAF BEHAVIOUR: Leafs have the original /24 connected subnet plus BGP-learned mini-subnet prefixes (/25s) pointing via borders through L3VNI. Local host /32 routes must be maintained — CEF removes Adj-sourced /32 entries when a more-specific subnet prefix (the mini-subnet) covers them. EVPN must install the host route into RIB

SELECTIVE FLOODING: With subnet slicing, flooding is restricted — no leaf-to-leaf, no leaf-to-border, no border-to-border flooding. Only border-to-leaf flooding is allowed for host discovery. Borders use the mini-subnet gateway address (not the original /24 gateway) for ARP/discovery

MICROLOOP PREVENTION: Sliced prefixes in CEF carry 'subnet-slice Y, microloop-check Y' flags. When a host /32 is missing on a leaf (due to control plane delay, link failure, or mobility), traffic matching the /25 sliced prefix from fabric SHOULD be denied by hardware (DENY_ROUTE) instead of forwarded back to the border. Without the hardware fix, a single packet generates ~252 looped copies before TTL expires

MICROLOOP VERIFICATION: Check 'show ip cef vrf <vrf> <prefix> internal' for 'FIB-FWD-Policy: subnet-slice Y, microloop-check Y'. Then check 'show platform software fed active ip route vrf <vrf> <prefix>' for 'Forwarding-Policy: microloop-check Y'. Finally check hardware enforcement: 'show platform hardware fed active fwd-asic drops exceptions | include DENY' — DENY_ROUTE counter should increment when microloop traffic is denied

MICROLOOP REPRODUCTION: To reproduce in a lab: (1) Verify /32 host route exists on leaf VTEP, (2) Start monitor capture on fabric uplink, (3) Remove /32 with 'test cef route ipv4 vrf <vrf> <host>/32 remove', (4) Send 1 ping from remote, (5) Stop capture, (6) Check capture for same ICMP id/seq with decrementing TTL. 252 copies from 1 ping = loop confirmed at ~126,000 loops/sec

MICROLOOP ROOT CAUSE: The microloop-check flag propagates through IOSd CEF -> FMAN-RP -> FMAN-FP -> FED software pipeline. The AAL -> ASIC hardware programming must set 'v4_v6_fwd_ad.microloop_check = 1' in aal_l3_prefix.c to program denyRoute=1 in the Blueshift ASIC forwarding adjacency. Without this, the ASIC forwards packets normally and the loop occurs

ANCHOR REDUNDANCY: Multiple borders can share the same mini-subnet index for redundancy (Redundancy Group). Example: B1 and B2 both have index [0], B3 and B4 both have index [1]. A single border can also own multiple indices if needed

ARP MULTI-SUBNET: Borders use the corresponding mini-subnet gateway address for ARP discovery. For /25 index 0, gateway is .126; for /25 index 1, gateway is .254. Hosts on leafs respond to ARP from either gateway. Device-tracking on leaf shows hosts learned via ARP from the appropriate mini-subnet gateway

runbook_d146698c.name: EVPN VXLAN S1 (Leaba) Platform Troubleshooting
runbook_d146698c.description: Silicon One (Leaba/S1) ASIC-specific EVPN VXLAN troubleshooting group. Run the parent 'EVPN VXLAN Common' workflow first to establish baseline health, then use this workflow for S1-based platforms (Cat9300X, Cat9400X, Cat9500X, Cat9600X with Silicon One ASICs). Includes S1-specific insight framework CLIs for hardware-level VXLAN verification: L2 multicast group membership, VXLAN port encapsulation, VNI encap programming, and NPU trap analysis. Drill into sub-workflows for: Protected VLAN ARP failure, L2VNI unicast forwarding, L3VNI inter-VLAN routing, BUM Ingress Replication, and BUM Underlay Multicast troubleshooting.
symptoms: Silicon One ASIC EVPN hardware drop
NPU trap incrementing for EVPN traffic
Protected VLAN ARP failure on S1 platform
S1 L2 multicast group missing VXLAN member

cli_commands.command: show platform hardware fed switch active fwd-asic insight npu_host_all_traps_data(npu_id=0)
cli_commands.purpose: S1 NPU: Dump all NPU trap counters on ASIC 0. Check ethernet-no-vni-mapping and ethernet-split-horizon for EVPN-related drops. Non-zero delta while reproducing confirms hardware is dropping packets before IOSd

cli_commands.command: show platform hardware fed switch active fwd-asic traps npu-traps asic 0
cli_commands.purpose: S1 NPU traps: Focused view of NPU trap counters. Run twice to measure delta during reproduction. Key traps: ethernet-no-vni-mapping (VNI encap missing), ethernet-split-horizon (protected VLAN drop)

cli_commands.command: show platform software fed sw active vni all
cli_commands.purpose: S1 FED: List all VNIs programmed in the FED software layer. Compare against 'show nve vni' to detect VNIs present in software but missing from S1 ASIC

cli_commands.command: show platform software fed switch active ifm interfaces vlan
cli_commands.purpose: S1 FED: Get IF_ID for each VLAN interface. Required to obtain the Mcast group GID (MCG GID) for S1 insight commands in sub-workflows

cli_commands.command: show platform hardware fed switch active fwd-asic resource tcam utilization
cli_commands.purpose: S1 TCAM: Full TCAM utilization. S1 platforms may have different TCAM sizes and regions than Doppler. Check Lisp-related rows for VNI capacity

analysis_points: Check NPU trap counters for ethernet-no-vni-mapping and ethernet-split-horizon — non-zero delta confirms S1 ASIC is dropping EVPN traffic
Compare FED VNI count against 'show nve vni' — mismatch means VNIs not programmed in S1 hardware
Get VLAN IF_IDs from FED ifm for subsequent S1 insight queries (l2m_group_members, vxlan_ports_dump, vxlan_vni_details)
After S1 baseline, select sub-workflow: S1 Platform Command for ASIC-level VXLAN inspection, or Protected VLAN ARP for NPU trap drops on protected VLANs

runbook_2c8aad25.name: EVPN Protected VLAN ARP Failure - NPU Trap Drop
runbook_2c8aad25.description: Diagnose ARP resolution failures in protected VLAN segments. NPU traps ethernet-no-vni-mapping and ethernet-split-horizon drop ARP before reaching IOSd. Use when ARP is not working on protected VLANs due to incorrect VNI/split-horizon reprogramming. Run 'EVPN VXLAN S1 (Leaba) Platform Troubleshooting' parent workflow first.

cli_commands.purpose: Check Protected flag and EVI state
Verify protected keyword on vlan config

cli_commands.command: show platform software fed switch active matm macTable vlan {vlan}
cli_commands.purpose: Check gateway MAC type and adj_id. Identify gateway MAC address for detailed lookup in next CLI

cli_commands.command: show platform software fed switch active matm macTable vlan {vlan} mac {gateway_mac} detail
cli_commands.purpose: Detailed hardware programming for gateway MAC. Shows L2 Service Port OID/GID and NPL hardware info per ASIC. Success: Port Type is VXLAN Port with Dest IP, Src IP, and VNI populated. Failure: 'Error in result: %error%Not found in mac table' after Dest/Src IP line means VNI encap is missing from hardware

interpretation_rules.pattern: Error in result:\s*%error%Not found in mac table
interpretation_rules.message: VNI encap is NOT programmed in hardware for this MAC. NPL lookup fails after Dest/Src IP resolution. Traffic to this remote MAC will be dropped by the ASIC.
interpretation_rules.recommendation: Remove and re-add the protected keyword on the VLAN to force FED to reprogram VNI encap entries.

interpretation_rules.message: VNI encap is correctly programmed in NPL hardware. VXLAN encapsulation path is healthy for this MAC.

cli_commands.command: show platform hardware fed switch active fwd-asic traps npu-traps asic 0
cli_commands.purpose: Check ethernet-no-vni-mapping and ethernet-split-horizon trap counters - run twice to measure delta
Check if gateway IP is resolved
Verify NVE tunnel to gateway RLOC is UP
Check VNI state and VLAN mapping
Cross-check IOS MAC table

analysis_points: CRITICAL: Check if Protected True on VTEP EVI but Protected False on CGW
CRITICAL: NPU traps ethernet-no-vni-mapping and ethernet-split-horizon must be checked for increments while reproducing
CRITICAL: Gateway MAC must be Type 0x1000001 LISP_REMOTE with valid adj_id
CRITICAL: macTable detail must show Port Type: VXLAN Port with VNI populated per ASIC. 'Error in result: %error%Not found in mac table' = VNI encap missing
Use EVPN S1 platform command workflow: run vxlan_vni_details to cross-verify VNI encap programming at ASIC level
If gateway IP missing from device-tracking then ARP never completed
Workaround: Remove and re-add protected keyword to force FED reprogram
Root cause: Remote MAC adjacency not reprogrammed when protected state changes on VLAN

runbook_1a0fc84b.name: EVPN VXLAN L2VNI Troubleshooting - S1 Platform
runbook_1a0fc84b.description: End-to-end L2 known unicast forwarding verification between VTEPs in the same L2VNI on Cat9350/S1 platforms. Walks the data-plane from SISF MAC learning through EVPN Manager, L2RIB, BGP route-type 2, L2FIB, FED matm/remote-MAC programming, SDK vxlan_ports_dump, and VNI encap/decap tunnel verification. Based on NPI training FEAT-32620 for 26.1.1. Run 'EVPN VXLAN S1 (Leaba) Platform Troubleshooting' parent workflow first.
symptoms: L2 unicast not forwarding between VTEPs in same VLAN
Remote MAC not learned on local VTEP
MAC missing from EVPN Manager or L2RIB
BGP route-type 2 not received or not imported
FED matm shows no RLOC for remote MAC
VNI encap/decap tunnel missing in SDK

cli_commands.purpose: SISF: Verify local MAC is learned. Only local MACs appear in SISF. Policy should be evpn-device-track. State MAC-REACHABLE confirms host is active

interpretation_rules.message: SISF has learned the local MAC with evpn-device-track policy.
Local MAC missing from SISF. EVPN Manager will not learn it. Check physical link, VLAN membership, and SISF policy binding.

cli_commands.purpose: EVPN Manager: Verify local MAC learned from SISF and remote MAC received from L2RIB. Local entry shows physical interface:vlan, remote entry shows VTEP tunnel loopback IP

interpretation_rules.message: EVPN Manager has local MAC. Will be pushed to L2RIB.
EVPN Manager has remote MAC via L2RIB from BGP.
MAC not in EVPN Manager. Check SISF learning for local or BGP peering for remote.

cli_commands.purpose: L2RIB: Verify local MAC from L2VPN producer and remote MAC from BGP producer. Local prefix Prod=L2VPN, remote prefix Prod=BGP with V:<vni> <remote-vtep-ip>

interpretation_rules.message: L2RIB has local MAC from EVPN Manager. Will distribute to BGP and L2FIB.
L2RIB has remote MAC from BGP. Will update EVPN Manager and L2FIB.
Remote MAC not in L2RIB. Check BGP l2vpn evpn peering and route-type 2 advertisement from remote VTEP.

cli_commands.purpose: BGP: Verify local MAC is advertised as route-type 2. Check locally sourced from 0.0.0.0, EVPN ESI, Label1 (VNI ID), Extended Community RT and ENCAP:8 (VxLAN), and Local irb vxlan vtep section for vrf, l3-vni, router-mac, core-irb, vtep-ip
BGP: Verify remote MAC route-type 2. Should show paths via RR with imported path in evi table. Check EVPN ESI, Label1 (VNI), RT, ENCAP:8, Originator, Cluster list

L2FIB: Verify remote MAC from BGP > L2RIB > L2FIB. Check Unicast Address table for VXLAN_UC entry with PL and T:VXLAN_UC [MAC]<vni>:<remote-vtep-ip>. Also check Flood List for VXLAN_REP entries for BUM replication

interpretation_rules.message: L2FIB has remote MAC with VXLAN unicast programming.
Remote MAC not in L2FIB. Check L2RIB entry and L2FIB programming. Possible FED/SDK issue.

cli_commands.command: show platform software fed switch active matm macTable vlan {vlan}
cli_commands.purpose: FED: Verify MAC routes programmed in FED. Remote MAC shows Type 0x1000001 with RLOC <remote-vtep-ip> and adj_id. Local MAC shows Type 0x1 with physical interface

cli_commands.command: show platform software fed switch active matm macTable vlan {vlan} mac {remote_mac} detail
cli_commands.purpose: FED: Verify remote MAC NPL hardware programming. Check L2 Service Port OID, Port Type: VXLAN Port, NPL Hardware Information showing Destination IP, Source IP, VNI. Must be programmed on all ASICs

interpretation_rules.message: Remote MAC VXLAN encap is correctly programmed in NPL hardware.
Remote MAC VXLAN programming missing in FED. Check NVE tunnel and VNI configuration.

cli_commands.command: show platform hardware fed switch active fwd-asic insight vxlan_ports_dump(gid={vxlan_port_gid},devid=0)
cli_commands.purpose: SDK: Verify VXLAN port encapsulation details. Check Tunnel Mode (ENCAP_ONLY for unicast), Dest IP (remote VTEP), Src IP (local VTEP), TTL/QoS Inheritance Mode

cli_commands.command: show platform hardware fed switch active fwd-asic insight vxlan_vni_details({vni},is_l2=True,is_encap=True)
cli_commands.purpose: SDK: Verify L2 VNI encap programming. Each remote VTEP and multicast group should appear with correct SWITCH GID matching VLAN ID

cli_commands.command: show platform software fed switch active ip route {remote_vtep_ip}/32 detail
cli_commands.purpose: FED: Verify underlay route to remote VTEP. Check ECMP paths, next-hop OIDs, outgoing port type (l3ac). Multiple paths indicate ECMP via spine uplinks

cli_commands.command: show platform software fed switch active ifm mappings gid {egress_gid}
cli_commands.purpose: FED: Map egress GID to physical interface (e.g., Port-channel to spine). Confirms underlay reachability path

cli_commands.command: show platform software fed switch active ifm interfaces efp
cli_commands.purpose: FED Decap: Extract IF_ID for nve1.VNI<vni> EFP interface on receiving VTEP. State must be Ready

cli_commands.command: show platform software fed switch active ifm if-id {efp_if_id}
cli_commands.purpose: FED Decap: Verify EFP subblock - Instance ID (VNI), Vlan Id, Type (L2_CP for L2VNI), Symmetric IRB, Core Vlan Id. Check NPL Hardware VNI-to-VLAN mapping

interpretation_rules.message: L2VNI decap EFP is correctly programmed.
L2VNI decap EFP is not properly configured. Check NVE member VNI and VLAN configuration mapping.

cli_commands.command: show platform hardware fed switch active fwd-asic insight vxlan_vni_details({vni},is_l2=True,is_encap=False)
cli_commands.purpose: SDK Decap: Verify decap tunnels - BUM decap tunnel (Dest 0.0.0.0, Src 224.0.0.0) and Unicast decap tunnel (Dest 0.0.0.0, Src <remote-vtep-ip>). Both must exist for L2VNI

interpretation_rules.message: L2VNI decap tunnels correctly programmed in SDK.
Missing unicast decap tunnel for remote VTEP. VNI decap will fail. Check NVE peer state and BGP route-type 3.

analysis_points: CRITICAL: Verify SISF learns local MAC with evpn-device-track policy. Missing SISF entry means EVPN Manager never gets the MAC
CRITICAL: L2RIB must show Prod=L2VPN for local and Prod=BGP for remote. If BGP entry missing, check BGP l2vpn evpn peering and route-type 2 advertisements
CRITICAL: L2FIB bridge-domain must have VXLAN_UC entry with correct VNI and remote VTEP IP for unicast forwarding
CRITICAL: FED matm detail must show Port Type: VXLAN Port with Dest IP, Src IP, VNI programmed per ASIC. Missing = encap not programmed
Verify SDK vxlan_ports_dump shows ENCAP_ONLY tunnel mode for unicast
Verify SDK vxlan_vni_details encap shows all remote VTEPs with correct SWITCH GID
Verify decap tunnels exist: BUM decap (0.0.0.0/224.0.0.0) and unicast decap (0.0.0.0/<remote-vtep-ip>)
Check underlay routing: FED ip route to remote VTEP must resolve via ECMP through spine uplinks. Missing route = no VXLAN reachability
Troubleshooting flow: SISF > EVPN Mgr > L2RIB > BGP RT-2 > L2FIB > FED matm > SDK vxlan_ports/vni > Underlay route > Decap tunnels

runbook_2d8d9dd5.name: EVPN VXLAN L3VNI Troubleshooting - S1 Platform
runbook_2d8d9dd5.description: End-to-end L3 inter-VLAN unicast forwarding verification between VTEPs in different VNIs (L3VNI / symmetric IRB) on Cat9350/S1 platforms. Walks the data-plane from SISF MAC/IP learning through EVPN Manager, L2RIB, BGP route-type 2 with dual labels (L2 + VRF), VPNv4 unicast VRF route, RIB, FED overlay route with VXLAN next-hop, SDK encap/decap tunnel verification including VRF GID and Router MAC. Based on NPI training FEAT-32620 for 26.1.1. Run 'EVPN VXLAN S1 (Leaba) Platform Troubleshooting' parent workflow first.
symptoms: L3 inter-VLAN traffic not forwarding between VTEPs
Remote host IP unreachable across VRF overlay
VPNv4 route missing Remote VxLAN section
RIB route not installed via core SVI
FED overlay route not using VXLAN next-hop

cli_commands.purpose: SISF: Verify local MAC/IP binding learned. ARP entry should show host IP, MAC, interface, VLAN, state REACHABLE. This is the starting point for EVPN type-2 MAC/IP route creation

cli_commands.command: show l2vpn evpn mac ip evi {evi}
cli_commands.purpose: EVPN Manager: Verify local MAC/IP learned. Shows IP Address, EVI, VLAN, MAC Address, and Next Hop (local interface:vlan)
L2RIB: Verify local MAC/IP from L2VPN producer. Shows EVI, Prod, MAC, Host IP, Next Hop. Local entries have Prod=L2VPN

cli_commands.command: show bgp l2vpn evpn evi {evi} route-type 2 0 {local_mac_no_dots} {local_ip}
cli_commands.purpose: BGP MAC VRF: Verify local MAC/IP advertised with dual labels. Label1 = L2VNI, Label2 = L3VNI (VRF). Extended Community must include VRF stitching RT as well as L2 RT. Check Router MAC and Local irb vxlan vtep section for vrf, l3-vni, core-irb

interpretation_rules.message: Both L2VNI and L3VNI labels present. Symmetric IRB is working.
L3VNI label missing. Check VRF definition, L3VNI member vni, and core SVI (Vlan901) configuration.

cli_commands.purpose: BGP VPNv4: Verify remote host IP stitched from EVPN type-2 into VPNv4. Check imported path from route-type 2, Remote VxLAN section showing Topoid, Remote Router MAC, Encap 8, Egress VNI (L3VNI), RTEP (remote VTEP IP)

interpretation_rules.message: VPNv4 route correctly stitched from EVPN type-2. Remote VxLAN section shows L3VNI encap parameters.
Remote IP missing from VPNv4 VRF. Check BGP address-family ipv4 vrf with 'advertise l2vpn evpn' and route-target import.

cli_commands.purpose: RIB: Verify remote host route in VRF. Should show 'Known via bgp', next-hop as remote VTEP IP via core SVI (Vlan901). This confirms the overlay route is installed in the routing table

interpretation_rules.message: L3VNI route correctly installed in RIB via core SVI.
Remote host route missing from VRF RIB. Check VPNv4 import and BGP best-path selection.

cli_commands.command: show platform software fed switch active ip route vrf {vrf} {remote_ip}/32
cli_commands.purpose: FED: Verify overlay route programming. Check l3_dest type la_vxlan_next_hop_base (VXLAN next-hop). NPL Hardware shows Destination type DESTINATION_L2_DLP, Dest/Src IP, VNI. ADJ section shows encap_type:VXLAN, overlay_vrf, vxlan_tunnel_gid

interpretation_rules.message: FED overlay route correctly points to VXLAN next-hop with L3VNI encapsulation.
FED overlay route not programmed as VXLAN next-hop. Check NVE member vni vrf configuration and core SVI state.

cli_commands.command: show platform hardware fed switch active fwd-asic insight vxlan_nexthop_details(devid=0,nhop_oid={nhop_oid})
cli_commands.purpose: SDK: Verify VXLAN next-hop OID maps to correct VXLAN port GID (l2-vxlan-port-gid). Each ASIC should have its own tunnel

cli_commands.command: show platform hardware fed switch active fwd-asic insight vxlan_ports_dump(gid={vxlan_port_gid},devid=0)
cli_commands.purpose: SDK: Verify encap tunnel details per ASIC. Must show ENCAP_ONLY mode, correct Dest IP (remote VTEP), Src IP (local VTEP)

cli_commands.command: show platform hardware fed switch active fwd-asic insight vxlan_vni_details({l3vni},is_l2=False,is_encap=True)
cli_commands.purpose: SDK: Verify L3VNI encap programming. Shows VRF GID, VNI, Src/Dest MAC (local/remote Router MAC), Encap Profile GID per remote VTEP. Router MAC must match BGP-advertised Router MAC attribute

interpretation_rules.message: L3VNI encap correctly programmed with Router MAC for VXLAN routing.
L3VNI encap programming error. Check core SVI MAC, VRF definition, and BGP Router MAC attribute.

cli_commands.command: show platform software fed switch active ip route {remote_vtep_ip}/32 detail
cli_commands.purpose: FED: Verify underlay ECMP route to remote VTEP. Check ecmp numpaths, next-hop MAC/OID, outgoing port type l3ac

cli_commands.command: show platform software fed switch active ifm mappings gid {egress_gid}
cli_commands.purpose: FED: Map egress GID to physical interface for underlay path verification

cli_commands.command: show platform software fed switch active ifm interfaces efp
cli_commands.purpose: FED Decap: Extract IF_ID for nve1.VNI<l3vni> EFP interface. Both L2VNI and L3VNI EFPs should be present

cli_commands.command: show platform software fed switch active ifm if-id {l3vni_efp_if_id}
cli_commands.purpose: FED Decap: Verify L3VNI EFP subblock - Type must be L3_CP (not L2_CP), Vrf Id must match VRF GID, Instance ID = L3VNI, Core Vlan Id, Core SVI If Id. NPL VNI-to-VLAN maps L3VNI to core VLAN

interpretation_rules.message: L3VNI decap EFP correctly programmed for symmetric IRB.
L3VNI EFP wrongly typed as L2_CP. Check 'member vni <vni> vrf <name>' under NVE interface.

cli_commands.command: show platform hardware fed switch active fwd-asic insight vxlan_vni_details({l3vni},is_l2=False,is_encap=False)
cli_commands.purpose: SDK Decap: Verify L3VNI decap tunnels. Shows VRF GID, VNI, Src/Dest MAC per remote VTEP. Each remote VTEP that can send L3VNI traffic should have a decap entry

analysis_points: CRITICAL: BGP route-type 2 must carry dual labels - Label1 (L2VNI) and Label2 (L3VNI/VRF). Missing Label2 means no L3VNI stitching
CRITICAL: VPNv4 must show 'imported path from RT-2' with Remote VxLAN section containing Egress VNI, RTEP, and Remote Router MAC
CRITICAL: FED overlay route must resolve to la_vxlan_next_hop_base (not regular la_next_hop). ADJ encap_type must be VXLAN with correct overlay_vrf
CRITICAL: SDK L3VNI encap must include Router MAC (Src/Dest MAC) for VXLAN routing. Src MAC = local core SVI MAC, Dest MAC = remote Router MAC from BGP
Verify core SVI (Vlan901) is up with 'ip unnumbered Loopback1', ipv6 enable, no autostate, and VRF forwarding assigned
Verify 'member vni <vni> vrf <name>' under NVE for L3VNI, and 'vlan configuration <core-vlan>' with 'member vni <l3vni>'
Check route-targets: VRF must have 'route-target export/import 1:1 stitching' and BGP address-family ipv4 vrf must have 'advertise l2vpn evpn'
L3VNI decap EFP Type must be L3_CP (not L2_CP). Type mismatch indicates wrong NVE member config (mcast-group vs vrf)
Troubleshooting flow: SISF MAC/IP > EVPN Mgr > L2RIB > BGP RT-2 (dual label) > VPNv4 > RIB > FED VXLAN NH > SDK encap/decap > Underlay ECMP

runbook_ce4bfb2c.name: EVPN VXLAN BUM Ingress Replication - S1 Platform
runbook_ce4bfb2c.description: Diagnose BUM (Broadcast/Unknown-Unicast/Multicast) traffic failures using Ingress Replication mode on Cat9350/S1 platforms. Use when BUM flooding is not working or broken. Walks the encap path from MAC table through L2 multicast group members, VXLAN port encap, and VNI encap entries. Run 'EVPN VXLAN S1 (Leaba) Platform Troubleshooting' parent workflow first.
symptoms: BUM traffic not reaching remote VTEPs with Ingress Replication
Unknown unicast flooding not working across VXLAN fabric
ARP requests not reaching remote hosts
L2 multicast group missing VXLAN member
VXLAN port GID not found for remote VTEP
VNI encap entries missing for Ingress Replication
Decap MCG not mapping to correct physical port

cli_commands.purpose: Verify local MAC is learned in MAC table. DYNAMIC type indicates data-plane learned host

cli_commands.command: show platform software fed switch active ifm interfaces vlan
cli_commands.purpose: IFM: Extract IF_ID for VLAN SVI. State must be Ready. This IF_ID is used to query the Mcast group GID

cli_commands.command: show platform software fed switch active ifm if-id {vlan_if_id}
cli_commands.purpose: IFM: Extract Mcast group GID from VLAN subblock. Shows OID, GID, Mcast group oid/Gid per ASIC, BD RM handle, and counter sets. The Mcast group Gid is needed for l2m_group_members query

interpretation_rules.message: VLAN has valid multicast group GID for BUM replication.
VLAN missing multicast group. BUM replication will not work. Check VLAN configuration and NVE member VNI.

cli_commands.command: show platform hardware fed switch active fwd-asic insight l2m_group_members(l2_mcg_gid="{mcg_gid}")
cli_commands.purpose: SDK: List L2 multicast group members. L2_AC type = local port (DENSE_AC dest). VXLAN type = remote VTEP replication tunnel. For Ingress Replication each remote VTEP has a VXLAN member. Extract l2-dest-gid for VXLAN type to verify encap details

interpretation_rules.message: L2 MCG has local port and VXLAN replication members. BUM will be sent to local ports and replicated to remote VTEPs.
No VXLAN member in L2 MCG. BUM traffic will not be replicated to the VXLAN fabric. Check NVE peer discovery and BGP route-type 3 (IMET).

cli_commands.command: show platform hardware fed switch active fwd-asic insight vxlan_ports_dump(gid={vxlan_port_gid})
cli_commands.purpose: SDK: Verify VXLAN port encapsulation for BUM replication. For Ingress Replication: Dest IP = specific remote VTEP IP, Tunnel Mode = ENCAP_ONLY

interpretation_rules.message: VXLAN port is configured for Ingress Replication with unicast encap to remote VTEP.
VXLAN port uses multicast destination. This is Underlay Multicast mode, not Ingress Replication. Check replication-type configuration.

cli_commands.command: show platform hardware fed switch active fwd-asic insight vxlan_vni_details({vni},is_l2=True,is_encap=True)
cli_commands.purpose: SDK: Verify L2VNI encap entries. Each entry shows Vxlan Port GID, Dest/Src IP, Encap Profile GID, SWITCH GID (= VLAN ID), VNI. For IR: one entry per remote VTEP with unicast Dest IP. May also show multicast entry if mixed mode

cli_commands.command: show platform software fed switch active ifm interfaces efp
cli_commands.purpose: FED Decap: On receiving VTEP, extract IF_ID for nve1.VNI<vni> EFP interface. State must be Ready

cli_commands.command: show platform software fed switch active ifm if-id {efp_if_id}
cli_commands.purpose: FED Decap: Verify EFP subblock - Instance ID (VNI), Vlan Id, Type (L2_CP), EVPN mcast grp gid. This EVPN mcast grp gid is the MCG on the decap side

cli_commands.command: show platform hardware fed switch active fwd-asic insight l2m_group_members(l2_mcg_gid="{decap_mcg_gid}")
cli_commands.purpose: SDK Decap: Verify decap MCG members. For IR decap, only L2_AC members (local ports in the VLAN). Extract sysport-gid to find the physical egress port

cli_commands.command: show platform software fed switch active ifm mappings gid {sysport_gid}
cli_commands.purpose: FED: Map sysport-gid from decap MCG to physical interface. Confirms traffic will egress on the correct port
Cross-check: Verify egress port is a member of the VLAN. Port missing from VLAN = traffic will be dropped after decap

analysis_points: CRITICAL: l2m_group_members must show VXLAN type member for each remote VTEP. Missing VXLAN member = no BUM replication to that VTEP
CRITICAL: For Ingress Replication, vxlan_ports_dump Dest IP must be a unicast remote VTEP address (not multicast). One tunnel per remote VTEP
CRITICAL: vxlan_vni_details encap must have entries for all remote VTEPs. SWITCH GID must equal VLAN ID
Config requirement: 'replication-type ingress' under 'l2vpn evpn' (global) or 'l2vpn evpn instance' (per-EVI). NVE uses 'member vni <vni> ingress-replication'
Decap side: EVPN mcast grp gid from EFP subblock drives the decap flood list. sysport-gid maps to the physical egress port - verify it matches VLAN port
Check BGP route-type 3 (IMET) exchange between VTEPs to confirm IR peer discovery is complete
Troubleshooting flow: MAC table > IFM VLAN IF_ID > Mcast GID > l2m_group_members > vxlan_ports_dump > vxlan_vni_details encap > Decap: EFP > MCG > sysport-gid > Interface > VLAN membership

runbook_c233ffe8.name: EVPN VXLAN BUM Underlay Multicast - S1 Platform
runbook_c233ffe8.description: BUM (Broadcast/Unknown-Unicast/Multicast) traffic verification using Underlay Multicast mode on Cat9350/S1 platforms. Walks the encap path: MAC table, IFM VLAN IF_ID, Mcast group GID, L2 multicast group members (l2m_group_members), VXLAN port encap with multicast Dest IP, VNI encap entries, and underlay IP multicast routing (ip mroute). For decap: EFP interface, Mcast group GID, sysport-gid to physical interface. Config uses 'replication-type static' with 'member vni <vni> mcast-group <mcast-ip>'. Based on NPI training FEAT-32620 for 26.1.1. Run 'EVPN VXLAN S1 (Leaba) Platform Troubleshooting' parent workflow first.
symptoms: BUM traffic not reaching remote VTEPs with Underlay Multicast
Unknown unicast flooding fails across VXLAN fabric
ARP requests not forwarded to remote VTEPs
VXLAN port encap shows wrong Dest IP (unicast instead of multicast)
L2 MCG missing VXLAN member for multicast replication
Decap MCG not mapping to physical port

cli_commands.purpose: Verify local MAC is learned in MAC table. DYNAMIC type indicates data-plane learned host

cli_commands.command: show platform software fed switch active ifm interfaces vlan
cli_commands.purpose: IFM: Extract IF_ID for VLAN SVI. State must be Ready

cli_commands.command: show platform software fed switch active ifm if-id {vlan_if_id}
cli_commands.purpose: IFM: Extract Mcast group GID from VLAN subblock per ASIC. Shows OID, GID, Stats GID, Mcast group oid/Gid, counter sets

cli_commands.command: show platform hardware fed switch active fwd-asic insight l2m_group_members(l2_mcg_gid="{mcg_gid}")
cli_commands.purpose: SDK: List L2 multicast group members. L2_AC type = local port. VXLAN type = multicast encap tunnel. For Underlay Multicast the VXLAN member Dest IP is the mcast-group address. Extract l2-dest-gid for VXLAN type

interpretation_rules.message: L2 MCG has VXLAN member using underlay multicast address.
VXLAN member uses unicast Dest IP. This is Ingress Replication mode, not Underlay Multicast. Check replication-type config.

cli_commands.command: show platform hardware fed switch active fwd-asic insight vxlan_ports_dump(gid={vxlan_port_gid})
cli_commands.purpose: SDK: Verify VXLAN port encapsulation. For Underlay Multicast: Dest IP = multicast group address (e.g., 226.0.0.1), Src IP = local VTEP IP, Tunnel Mode = ENCAP_ONLY

interpretation_rules.message: VXLAN port correctly configured for Underlay Multicast encap.
VXLAN port uses unicast Dest IP. Not Underlay Multicast. Check NVE 'member vni <vni> mcast-group <addr>'.

cli_commands.command: show platform hardware fed switch active fwd-asic insight vxlan_vni_details({vni},is_l2=True,is_encap=True)
cli_commands.purpose: SDK: Verify VNI encap entries. For Underlay Multicast: entry with multicast Dest IP and Encap Profile GID 0 (multicast). May also show unicast entries for known-unicast remote VTEPs

Underlay: Verify IP multicast routing for the mcast-group. Incoming interface = source loopback (Loopback1). Outgoing interface list = uplinks to spine. Flags should include 'x' (VxLAN group) and 'T' (SPT-bit set). Missing OIL = BUM traffic will not reach remote VTEPs

interpretation_rules.message: Underlay multicast route has valid outgoing interfaces. BUM traffic will reach remote VTEPs via multicast.
No outgoing interfaces for multicast group. BUM traffic will not leave this VTEP. Check PIM neighbor state, RP config, and multicast routing.

cli_commands.command: show platform software fed switch active ifm interfaces efp
cli_commands.purpose: FED Decap: On receiving VTEP, extract IF_ID for nve1.VNI<vni> EFP interface

cli_commands.command: show platform software fed switch active ifm if-id {efp_if_id}
cli_commands.purpose: FED Decap: Verify EFP subblock - Instance ID (VNI), Vlan Id, Type (L2_CP), EVPN mcast grp gid for decap flood list

cli_commands.command: show platform hardware fed switch active fwd-asic insight l2m_group_members(l2_mcg_gid="{decap_mcg_gid}")
cli_commands.purpose: SDK Decap: Verify decap MCG members. For Underlay Multicast decap, only L2_AC members (local ports). Extract sysport-gid

cli_commands.command: show platform software fed switch active ifm mappings gid {sysport_gid}
cli_commands.purpose: FED: Map sysport-gid to physical interface for decap egress
Cross-check: Verify egress port is a member of the VLAN

analysis_points: CRITICAL: l2m_group_members VXLAN member Dest IP must be a multicast address (e.g., 226.0.0.1). Unicast address means wrong replication-type config
CRITICAL: 'show ip mroute' must have valid Outgoing Interface List with spine uplinks. Empty OIL = underlay multicast tree not built. Check PIM, RP, and IGMP/PIM joins from NVE
CRITICAL: vxlan_ports_dump must show Dest IP = mcast-group address configured under NVE member VNI
Config requirement: 'replication-type static' under 'l2vpn evpn' (global). NVE uses 'member vni <vni> mcast-group <addr>'. Per-EVI can override
Difference from IR: Underlay Multicast uses a single VXLAN tunnel to the multicast group. The underlay multicast tree handles distribution. IR uses one tunnel per remote VTEP
Decap path is identical to IR: EFP > MCG > sysport-gid > physical interface
Verify underlay PIM/IGMP: spine must be RP or PIM-SM neighbor. NVE source loopback is the multicast source. Flags 'FTx' in mroute confirm VxLAN group
Troubleshooting flow: MAC table > IFM VLAN IF_ID > Mcast GID > l2m_group_members (mcast dest) > vxlan_ports_dump (mcast) > vxlan_vni_details encap > ip mroute > Decap: EFP > MCG > Interface > VLAN

runbook_fe960193.name: EVPN S1 platform command
runbook_fe960193.description: Silicon One (S1) ASIC-specific platform insight commands for EVPN VXLAN troubleshooting. These commands query the S1 SDK directly via the insight framework to inspect hardware-level VXLAN programming: L2 multicast group membership, VXLAN port encapsulation details, and VNI encap entries. Use when Doppler-era FED software commands are insufficient or to get S1-native ASIC details. Can be used alongside any EVPN sub-workflow for S1 hardware visibility.
symptoms: Need S1 ASIC-level VXLAN debugging beyond FED software commands
VXLAN adjacency needs hardware-level verification in S1 ASIC
L2 multicast group membership needs verification in S1 hardware
VXLAN port encapsulation details needed from S1 ASIC
VNI encap entries not programmed in S1 hardware

cli_commands.command: show platform hardware fed switch active fwd-asic insight l2m_group_members(l2_mcg_gid="{mcg_gid}")
cli_commands.purpose: Dump L2 multicast group members for a VLAN by MCG GID. Shows L2_AC (local port) and VXLAN members. Missing VXLAN member means BUM traffic is not replicated to the VXLAN fabric for that VLAN

cli_commands.command: show platform hardware fed switch active fwd-asic insight vxlan_ports_dump(gid={vxlan_port_gid})
cli_commands.purpose: Dump VXLAN port details by GID. Shows Dest IP (remote VTEP or multicast group), Src IP (local VTEP), tunnel mode, and encap profile. Confirms the VXLAN encapsulation path in the S1 ASIC

cli_commands.command: show platform hardware fed switch active fwd-asic insight "vxlan_vni_details({vni},is_l2=True,is_encap=True)"
cli_commands.purpose: Verify VNI L2 encapsulation is programmed in the S1 ASIC. Success: rows with Vxlan Port GID, Dest IP, Src IP, Encap Profile GID, SWITCH GID, VNI populated. Failure: empty table with only headers (VNI encap not programmed)

interpretation_rules.message: No VNI encap entries found - VNI is not programmed in S1 ASIC. VXLAN traffic will be silently dropped.
interpretation_rules.recommendation: Check FED programming path. For protected VLAN issues, remove and re-add the protected keyword to force FED to reprogram VNI encap entries.

analysis_points: Use l2m_group_members to verify both L2_AC and VXLAN members exist in the L2 multicast group for a VLAN. Missing VXLAN member = BUM not replicated
Use vxlan_ports_dump to confirm VXLAN port Dest/Src IP and tunnel mode
Use vxlan_vni_details with is_l2=True,is_encap=True to verify VNI encap programming. Empty table = VNI not programmed in S1 ASIC

runbook_54d215ae.name: EVPN Role-Aware Sub-Workflow: Leaf
runbook_54d215ae.description: Health check for an EVPN VXLAN leaf (VTEP) — verifies NVE up/up, all expected VNIs programmed in software and FED, BGP EVPN peering with both spines established, and remote VTEP peers reachable.  Selected automatically by the engine when the target device classifies as 'leaf' (has NVE, no route-reflector-clients, no external connectivity).
symptoms: NVE oper down on a leaf
BGP EVPN peering missing on a leaf
Tenant traffic black-holed at the leaf

cli_commands.purpose: Confirm NVE Admin/Oper Up with valid source loopback

interpretation_rules.message: NVE interface is not Operationally Up on this leaf — VXLAN encap/decap is broken.
interpretation_rules.recommendation: Check 'show running-config interface nve1' for missing source-interface or host-reachability.

cli_commands.purpose: Verify every L2/L3 VNI is in Up state
Verify remote VTEPs are Up with the correct L2CP/L3CP modes
Verify BGP EVPN peering to spines is Established with prefixes

cli_commands.command: show platform software fed sw active vni all
cli_commands.purpose: Confirm VNIs are programmed in FED (catches silent SW/HW skew)

cli_commands.command: show platform hardware fed switch active fwd-asic resource tcam utilization | include Lisp
cli_commands.purpose: Lisp Inst Mapping TCAM headroom — full TCAM silently drops VNIs
Local + remote MAC entries indicate end-to-end overlay learn

analysis_points: NVE must be Admin Up / Oper Up with the configured source loopback
Every VNI must show Up in 'show nve vni' AND be present in FED
Both spines must be Established neighbors in 'show bgp l2vpn evpn summary'
No route-reflector-client config is expected on a leaf

runbook_6c5ba6dd.name: EVPN Role-Aware Sub-Workflow: Spine
runbook_6c5ba6dd.description: Health check for an EVPN VXLAN spine (route-reflector) — verifies BGP EVPN RR-client neighbors are Established, the spine has no NVE/L2 VNIs (or only L3 VNI for RT5-only forwarding), and the underlay (OSPF/IS-IS) is fully adjacent.  Selected automatically when the engine classifies the device as 'spine' (no NVE or NVE with only L3VNI, AND has route-reflector-clients, AND no external connectivity).
symptoms: Leaf-to-leaf overlay reachability missing while leaves themselves are healthy
BGP EVPN peering on the spine flapping or stuck Active
RR not reflecting Type-2 / Type-5 routes between leaves

cli_commands.purpose: Every leaf RR-client must be Established with non-zero prefixes

interpretation_rules.message: At least one RR-client is NOT Established — leaves downstream of this spine will miss EVPN routes.
interpretation_rules.recommendation: Check 'show bgp l2vpn evpn neighbors <ip>' for the non-Established peer; verify update-source loopback and route-reflector-client config (direct or inherited via peer-policy template).

cli_commands.purpose: Confirm route-reflector-client coverage (direct or via peer-policy template)
Underlay OSPF adjacencies to all leaves must be Full
Underlay routing table sanity (OSPF/BGP path counts)
Spine should reflect Type-2/3/5 routes between leaves

analysis_points: Every leaf neighbor must be Established and reflecting prefixes
Spine should NOT have L2 VNIs (NVE absent or L3-only)
Underlay OSPF/IS-IS must be Full to all directly-attached leaves
route-reflector-client must cover ALL leaf neighbors — including those configured via 'neighbor X inherit peer-policy <name>' where the policy template carries 'route-reflector-client'

runbook_25abc16f.name: EVPN Role-Aware Sub-Workflow: Border-Leaf
runbook_25abc16f.description: Health check for an EVPN VXLAN border-leaf — combines the standard leaf VTEP checks with external-edge verification (eBGP / VRF-Lite handoff / DCI / Multi-Site).  Selected automatically when the engine classifies the device as 'border-leaf' (has NVE, no RR-clients, AND has external connectivity).
symptoms: External destinations unreachable from tenant VRFs
eBGP / VRF-Lite peer flap on the border
EVPN Type-5 routes missing for external prefixes

cli_commands.purpose: Confirm NVE Admin/Oper Up — same as leaf check
Verify L2/L3 VNIs are Up
Verify remote VTEP peers are Up
EVPN peering to spines must be Established
External eBGP neighbors must be Established

cli_commands.command: show ip route vrf all summary
cli_commands.purpose: Tenant VRF route counts; check for unexpected drops

cli_commands.command: show ip bgp vpnv4 all summary
cli_commands.purpose: If VPNv4 handoff is in use, confirm peering health
Confirm address-family ipv4 vrf <tenant> redistribution and route-targets

analysis_points: Border-leaf must satisfy ALL leaf invariants AND external edge invariants
External BGP peers (eBGP, VPNv4) must be Established
VRF-Lite handoff interfaces must be in the correct tenant VRF
Type-5 (IP-Prefix) advertisements should be observed for external prefixes

runbook_00391027.name: EVPN Role-Aware Sub-Workflow: Border-Spine
runbook_00391027.description: Health check for an EVPN VXLAN border-spine — combines the spine RR-client checks with external-edge verification (eBGP / VRF-Lite / DCI / Multi-Site).  Selected automatically when the engine classifies the device as 'border-spine' (route-reflector-clients configured, AND external connectivity).  The role identifier resolves RR-client status across both direct neighbor config and 'inherit peer-policy <template>' inheritance — a misclassification of Spine-01 (10.197.153.224) as border-leaf was the original trigger for this role-aware flow.
symptoms: Leaves not receiving EVPN reflected routes for external prefixes
External handoff (Multi-Site / DCI / VRF-Lite) broken at the spine layer
eBGP peer flap on a route-reflecting spine
RR-client misconfigured via peer-policy template (was reported as misclassified to border-leaf)

cli_commands.purpose: Every leaf RR-client must be Established
Confirm route-reflector-client coverage including peer-policy templates
External eBGP neighbors must be Established
Underlay OSPF must be Full to all attached leaves

cli_commands.command: show ip route vrf all summary
cli_commands.purpose: Tenant VRFs reachable via external edge

cli_commands.command: show ip bgp vpnv4 all summary
cli_commands.purpose: VPNv4 handoff health (if Multi-Site / VRF-Lite)

analysis_points: Border-spine must satisfy ALL spine invariants AND external edge invariants
RR-client coverage MUST include neighbors that inherit a peer-policy template containing 'route-reflector-client' — direct-only inspection misses these
External BGP peers (eBGP, VPNv4) must be Established

runbook_bc1280bd.name: EVPN Role-Aware Health Check
runbook_bc1280bd.description: Top-level EVPN VXLAN health check that automatically selects the correct verification path per device based on its detected fabric role (leaf, spine, border-leaf, border-spine).  Single-device callers can target this runbook directly via execute_troubleshooting_runbook — the engine classifies the device on the fly (live or from the latest ingested log bundle) and chains ONLY the role-matching sub.  Multi-device callers can pass an explicit device_role_map keyed by leaf*, spine*, border_leaf*, border_spine*, OR pass a list of IPs / bundle_ids and let the engine classify and build the map automatically.
symptoms: Verify EVPN fabric health without knowing each device's role up front
Run the right CLIs on each device based on its role (leaf / spine / border-leaf / border-spine)
Re-verify Spine-01 / 10.197.153.224 after the route-reflector-client via peer-policy template misclassification fix
analysis_points: The mother itself runs no CLIs — it only orchestrates role-typed subs
The engine's EVPN-gated branch in _chain_sub_runbooks_if_needed (single-device) and execute_multi_device_runbook (multi-device) performs the role classification before dispatch
Roles map to sub_runbooks via device_role: leaf -> leaf sub, spine -> spine sub, border-leaf -> border-leaf sub, border-spine -> border-spine sub

required_device_roles.label: EVPN Spine (Route-Reflector)
required_device_roles.description: Route-reflector — RR-clients configured (direct OR via peer-policy template), no external edge.

required_device_roles.label: EVPN Border-Leaf

runbook_c03ed4f4.name: IPsec Common Baseline
runbook_c03ed4f4.description: Common baseline IPsec troubleshooting workflow. Run these CLIs first for any IPsec / crypto issue to establish tunnel status, IKE peering, and SA state, then drill into a specific sub-workflow based on the symptom. Sub-workflows: IKEv2 SA Troubleshooting, IPsec SA and Rekey Validation, Tunnel Interface Diagnostics, IPsec Platform and Hardware Diagnostics.
symptoms: VRF routing table missing or empty
SVI-sourced tunnel up but traffic fails

cli_commands.purpose: Quick overview of all crypto sessions — shows peer, tunnel interface, IKEv2 SA status, and child (IPsec) SA status in a compact format. Look for Session status: UP-ACTIVE

interpretation_rules.message: Crypto session is NOT UP-ACTIVE — tunnel is down or negotiation incomplete
interpretation_rules.recommendation: If DOWN-NEGOTIATING: check underlay VRF reachability FIRST (Underlay and VRF Diagnostics sub-runbook) — the most common cause is no route to the peer, not crypto misconfiguration. If UP-IDLE or UP-NO-IKE: check IKEv2 SA state. If all tunnels fail simultaneously, suspect shared underlay issue

cli_commands.purpose: Detailed view of all crypto sessions including IKEv2 SA lifetime, IPsec SA inbound/outbound SPIs, encaps/decaps counters, and transform sets. Essential for identifying rekey issues and traffic flow

Per-tunnel detailed crypto session — IKEv2 SA and IPsec child SA state, SPIs, encaps/decaps, transform-set, and lifetime for a specific tunnel interface. Use when multiple tunnels exist and you need to isolate one
Compact one-line per-tunnel crypto session summary — quick status check (UP-ACTIVE/DOWN) for a specific tunnel without the full detail output
Summary count of all IPsec SAs — total, active, rekeying, and dead SAs. High dead SA count indicates rekey or cleanup issues; useful for quick health check
Verify IKEv2 SA state — must be READY. Shows local/remote addresses, FVRFs, IVRFs, SA status, and encryption/integrity algorithms negotiated

interpretation_rules.message: IKEv2 SA is not in READY state — Phase 1 negotiation has not completed successfully
interpretation_rules.recommendation: Run IKEv2 SA Troubleshooting sub-workflow. Check proposal/policy mismatch, authentication failure, or network reachability

cli_commands.purpose: Display all IPsec Security Associations — inbound/outbound SPIs, encaps/decaps packet counters, local/remote crypto endpoints, transform set, and per-SA status

interpretation_rules.message: Zero encapsulated packets — no outbound traffic flowing through this IPsec SA
interpretation_rules.recommendation: Verify interesting traffic matches the crypto ACL or tunnel routing. Check for routing pointing to the tunnel interface

interpretation_rules.message: Zero decapsulated packets — no inbound traffic being received on this IPsec SA
interpretation_rules.recommendation: Check remote peer SA state, verify reverse routing, and confirm peer is sending traffic

interpretation_rules.message: Outbound SPI is 0x0 — no active outbound IPsec SA installed. Tunnel cannot send encrypted traffic
interpretation_rules.recommendation: IPsec Phase 2 (child SA) has not been established. Check IKEv2 SA state first, then verify transform-set and crypto ACL

cli_commands.purpose: List all VRFs with their interfaces — verify FVRF and IVRF exist with correct interface assignments. If the FVRF has no interfaces, underlay routing is broken and no tunnel can establish. Quick sanity check before drilling into tunnel-specific diagnostics

interpretation_rules.message: FVRF has no interfaces — underlay routing context has no connectivity
interpretation_rules.recommendation: Device may have lost config after reboot. Check for backup config on flash and restore VRF interface assignments

cli_commands.command: show ip interface brief | include Tunnel
cli_commands.purpose: Quick check that all expected tunnel interfaces are up/up. A tunnel showing down/down indicates tunnel source/dest reachability or keepalive issues

interpretation_rules.message: Tunnel interface is down — cannot pass traffic
interpretation_rules.recommendation: Check tunnel source and destination reachability. Verify tunnel keepalives if configured

cli_commands.purpose: Review full crypto configuration: IKEv2 proposals, policies, profiles, keyrings, IPsec transform-sets, crypto maps or tunnel protection profiles, and pre-shared keys (obfuscated)
IKEv2 subsystem statistics — SA init/resp counts, rekey counters, delete notifications, and error counters. Key indicator for systemic negotiation failures
Display active crypto engine connections showing encryption/decryption byte counters per SA. Confirms crypto engine is processing traffic
Check for recent syslog messages related to crypto/IPsec/IKEv2 — negotiation failures, SA expiry, rekey errors, DPD timeouts, or certificate issues

analysis_points: Crypto session must show Session status: UP-ACTIVE for healthy tunnel

TRIAGE PRIORITY: If session shows DOWN-NEGOTIATING, check underlay VRF reachability FIRST — run 'IPsec Underlay and VRF Diagnostics' sub-runbook before IKEv2/IPsec SA sub-runbooks. The most common cause of DOWN-NEGOTIATING is no route to the peer, not crypto misconfiguration

SIMULTANEOUS FAILURE: When ALL tunnels go DOWN-NEGOTIATING at the same time, the root cause is shared underlay infrastructure — OSPF neighbor loss, FVRF interface removal, transit router failure, or VRF config wiped after reboot. Individual tunnel failures point to per-tunnel crypto issues
IKEv2 SA must be in READY state with correct local/remote addresses
IPsec SA must have non-zero outbound SPI and incrementing encaps/decaps
All expected Tunnel interfaces must be up/up in show ip interface brief
VRF CHECK: Verify FVRF has interfaces assigned in 'show ip vrf'. An FVRF with no interfaces means the underlay routing context is broken
Crypto config must have matching IKEv2 proposal, policy, profile, and keyring on both peers
Check show crypto ikev2 stats for error counters — high values indicate systemic negotiation issues
Review syslog for CRYPTO/IKEV2/IPSEC messages for recent failures

TUNNEL SOURCE AWARENESS: Note the source type for each tunnel — Loopback (most reliable), physical interface, or SVI. SVI-sourced tunnels have a known FED race condition (CSCwt84307) that can cause traffic failure despite tunnel showing UP

After running common CLIs, select a sub-runbook based on the specific symptom:
  - DOWN-NEGOTIATING → Underlay and VRF Diagnostics
  - IKEv2 SA issues → IKEv2 SA Troubleshooting
  - SA/rekey issues → IPsec SA and Rekey Validation
  - Tunnel interface issues → Tunnel Interface Diagnostics
  - Hardware/FED issues → Platform and Hardware Diagnostics
  - Stack issues → Stack Diagnostics
  - Config removal → Configuration Cleanup

runbook_b7201f55.name: IKEv2 SA Troubleshooting
runbook_b7201f55.description: Focused diagnostic for IKEv2 (Phase 1) SA negotiation issues including proposal/policy mismatch, authentication failures, profile misconfiguration, certificate problems, and DPD. Run 'IPsec Common Baseline' parent workflow first.
symptoms: IKEv2 SA not reaching READY state

cli_commands.purpose: Detailed IKEv2 SA state including session-id, SA lifetime, remaining lifetime, DPD configured interval/retry, encryption/integrity/PRF/DH group negotiated, NAT-T status, and rekey timestamps

interpretation_rules.message: IKEv2 SA remaining lifetime is below 5 minutes — rekey should occur soon. If it does not, rekey is likely failing
interpretation_rules.recommendation: Monitor for successful rekey. If SA expires without rekey, check IKEv2 stats for rekey failure counters

cli_commands.purpose: Display all configured IKEv2 proposals — encryption algorithms, integrity algorithms, PRF, and DH groups. Must match peer's proposal for negotiation to succeed
Display IKEv2 policies binding proposals to match criteria. Verify policy priority and attached proposal name
Display IKEv2 profiles — match criteria (address/identity), authentication method (pre-share/rsa-sig/eap), keyring reference, lifetime settings, DPD settings, and NAT keepalive configuration
IKEv2 statistics — SA init/resp success/fail counts, rekey success/fail, delete counts, invalid SPI, auth failures. Non-zero failure counts point to negotiation issues

interpretation_rules.message: IKEv2 SA init failures detected — initiator-side negotiation has failed at least once
interpretation_rules.recommendation: Check proposal/policy mismatch, pre-shared key mismatch, or network reachability. Enable debug crypto ikev2 for details

interpretation_rules.message: IKEv2 authentication failures detected — pre-shared key or certificate mismatch between peers
interpretation_rules.recommendation: Verify keyring pre-shared key matches on both peers. For certificate auth, verify PKI trust chain and certificate validity

cli_commands.purpose: Display configured keyrings — peer address bindings and pre-shared key references (keys are obfuscated). Verify correct keyring is bound to the IKEv2 profile
Display PKI certificates — issuer, subject, validity dates, serial number. Expired or untrusted certificates cause IKEv2 auth failures for RSA-sig authentication
Verify trustpoint enrollment status and certificate chain. Trustpoint must show Granted for CA and identity certificates
Display active IKEv2 sessions — session ID, SA status, local/remote identities, tunnel interface binding, and child SA count per session. Complements show crypto ikev2 sa with session-oriented view

Per-exchange-type IKEv2 statistics — IKE_SA_INIT, IKE_AUTH, CREATE_CHILD_SA, and INFORMATIONAL message counts for sent/received/failed. Pinpoints which IKEv2 exchange phase is failing (init vs auth vs child-SA)

interpretation_rules.message: IKE_SA_INIT exchange failures — Phase 1 initial handshake failing (proposal mismatch or reachability)
interpretation_rules.recommendation: Verify IKEv2 proposal/policy matches between peers and UDP 500 is not filtered

interpretation_rules.message: CREATE_CHILD_SA exchange failures — IPsec SA creation or rekey is failing
interpretation_rules.recommendation: Check transform-set and PFS group match. This exchange is used for both initial child SA and rekey operations

cli_commands.purpose: IKEv2 external service statistics — AAA, PKI, EAP, and other external service interaction counters. Non-zero failures here indicate issues with authentication backends (RADIUS/TACACS, certificate servers, etc.)
Display IKEv2 diagnostic error history — shows the most recent negotiation failure reasons with timestamps and peer addresses. Directly identifies root cause

Display IKEv2 authorization policies — route accept/set, ACL, IP pool, DNS/WINS, and other AAA attributes applied during IKEv2 authorization. Required context when the IKEv2 profile uses 'aaa authorization group' or policy-pushed routes. For named lookups use 'show crypto ikev2 authorization policy <policy-name>' (e.g. 'default')

Detailed per-session IKEv2 view — per-session encryption / integrity / PRF / DH / auth, full local & remote SPI pairs, child-SA traffic selectors, and Life/Active timers. More granular than 'show crypto ikev2 session' (order 8); use when auditing actually-negotiated parameters per peer or diagnosing CHILD_SA mis-selection. NOTE on Argon ASIC the keyword is 'detailed' (not 'detail')

analysis_points: IKEv2 SA must be in READY state with correct local/remote addresses
IKEv2 proposal must match between peers — encryption, integrity, PRF, and DH group must all agree
IKEv2 profile must have correct match criteria (address or identity) and authentication method
Pre-shared key in keyring must match exactly on both peers
For certificate auth, verify certificates are not expired and trust chain is complete
Check DPD settings — aggressive DPD with short intervals on unreliable links causes tunnel flaps
NAT-T must be detected and enabled if either peer is behind NAT
