These commands expose the hardware-level NetFlow programming including FNF profiles, masks, TCAM entries, and record counts in the ASIC. Run 'NetFlow Common Baseline' parent workflow first.
symptoms: Hardware vs software flow count mismatch
FED SW stats show export failures
ASIC NetFlow debug counters show errors
NetFlow ACL not attached to interface in ASIC
Hardware flow records not matching software cache
NetFlow resource allocation exhausted in ASIC
LEABA SDK FNF table entries missing or inconsistent
Silicon One SDK monitor or bind table mismatch

cli_commands.command: show platform software fed switch active fnf record-count all-asic
cli_commands.purpose: ASIC-level flow record counts — shows total number of hardware flow entries across all ASICs. Compare against software cache to detect hardware/software sync issues

cli_commands.command: show platform software fed switch active fnf monitors dump
cli_commands.purpose: FED-level dump of all NetFlow monitors including their hardware programming state. Verify the monitor is programmed into the forwarding plane

cli_commands.command: show platform software fed switch active fnf profiles-dump
cli_commands.purpose: FED NetFlow profile dump — shows the hardware profiles created for each flow record/monitor combination. Profiles define which packet fields are extracted in hardware

cli_commands.command: show platform software fed switch active fnf profile-maps-dump
cli_commands.purpose: Profile-to-monitor mapping in the FED — verifies the association between hardware profiles and software monitors

cli_commands.command: show platform software fed switch active fnf attach-points-dump
cli_commands.purpose: FED attach point dump — shows which interfaces have NetFlow monitors attached in hardware and in which direction (ingress/egress). Missing attach points mean the monitor is not programmed on that interface

interpretation_rules.message: No attach points found — NetFlow is not programmed on any interface in hardware
interpretation_rules.recommendation: Re-apply the flow monitor on the interface. If the issue persists, check for FED errors in 'show logging' and verify TCAM resources

cli_commands.command: show platform software fed switch active fnf fmask-entry all-asic
cli_commands.purpose: FNF flow mask entries in the ASIC — shows the flow mask programmed per monitor that determines which packet fields are used as cache keys in hardware

cli_commands.command: show platform software fed switch active fnf sw-stats-show
cli_commands.purpose: SW-side FNF statistics — cache hits, inserts, export attempts/failures. Key for debugging the software path. Compare export attempts vs failures to detect drops between FED and the exporter

interpretation_rules.message: FED-level FNF export failures detected — flow data is being lost in the software path
interpretation_rules.recommendation: Check exporter configuration and collector reachability. Cross-reference with 'show flow exporter statistics' for IOS-level failures

cli_commands.command: show platform software fed switch active fnf sw-stats-clear
cli_commands.purpose: Clears FED FNF SW stats counters — ALWAYS run this before a new repro attempt or bug capture to get a clean baseline. After clearing, reproduce the issue, then re-run sw-stats-show to see only the relevant delta counters

cli_commands.command: show platform software object-manager sw ac F0 statistics
cli_commands.purpose: Object-manager statistics for the active switch F0 processor — look for high failure counts or object leaks related to FNF objects. Persistent failures or growing pending counts indicate FED-to-ASIC programming issues that may affect NetFlow operation

interpretation_rules.message: Object-manager has non-zero failure counts — FNF objects may not be programmed correctly in hardware
interpretation_rules.recommendation: Check for FED errors in 'show logging'. If failures persist, a platform restart may be needed or engage TAC

cli_commands.command: show platform hardware fed switch active fwd-asic resource tcam table nfl_acl format 0
cli_commands.purpose: TCAM table for NetFlow ACLs — shows the TCAM entries allocated for FNF classification. Exhausted TCAM space prevents new monitors from being programmed in hardware

cli_commands.command: show platform software fed switch active fnf exporter-stats all-exporters
cli_commands.purpose: Platform statistics for FNF exporters from the hardware — shows Monitor ID, Exporter ID, Packet Count, and Records Sent per exporter. Verifies exporters are sending data at the hardware level. Compare with IOS-level 'show flow exporter statistics' to detect mismatches

interpretation_rules.message: FED exporter shows zero Records Sent — hardware is not exporting flow records despite having packet counts
interpretation_rules.recommendation: If Packet Count is non-zero but Records Sent is zero, the exporter pipeline is stuck. Check sw-stats-show for errors and verify exporter configuration

cli_commands.command: show platform software fed switch active fnf exporter-stats all-id
cli_commands.purpose: FED exporter statistics indexed by exporter ID — correlates with monitor configuration for multi-exporter setups

cli_commands.command: show platform hardware fed switch active fwd-asic insight acl attachment-circuit app-type NETFLOW
cli_commands.purpose: Insight into ASIC-level ACL attachment circuits for NETFLOW — shows which interfaces have NETFLOW ACL entries attached in the forwarding ASIC. Missing entries indicate the NetFlow monitor was not programmed onto the interface in hardware
Display dense ethernet port ACL entries for NETFLOW application type — shows per-port ACL bindings used by the NETFLOW classification pipeline in the ASIC

cli_commands.command: show platform hardware fed switch active fwd-asic insight netflow_acl_table_def()
cli_commands.purpose: Insight into the ASIC NetFlow ACL table definitions — shows the ACL table structure and entries programmed for FNF classification in the forwarding ASIC

cli_commands.command: show platform hardware fed switch active fwd-asic insight netflow_config_info_profiles()
cli_commands.purpose: Insight into ASIC-level NetFlow profile configuration — shows the FNF profiles programmed in the ASIC including which flow fields are being extracted by each profile

cli_commands.command: show platform hardware fed switch active fwd-asic insight netflow_config_info_resource_allocation()
cli_commands.purpose: Insight into ASIC NetFlow resource allocation — shows memory and table resources allocated for FNF in the forwarding ASIC. Helps identify resource exhaustion preventing new monitors from being programmed

cli_commands.command: show platform hardware fed switch active fwd-asic insight netflow_data_info()
cli_commands.purpose: Insight into ASIC NetFlow data information — shows the current state of flow data tables in the forwarding ASIC including entry counts and utilization

cli_commands.command: show platform hardware fed switch active fwd-asic insight netflow_debug_counters()
cli_commands.purpose: Insight into ASIC NetFlow debug counters — shows hardware-level flow creation, deletion, export, and error counters directly from the forwarding ASIC. Non-zero error counters indicate ASIC-level issues

interpretation_rules.message: ASIC-level NetFlow debug counters show errors — hardware flow processing is encountering issues
interpretation_rules.recommendation: Cross-reference with sw-stats-show and netflow_data_info() to isolate the failure point. Persistent errors may require TAC engagement

cli_commands.command: show platform hardware fed switch active fwd-asic insight netflow_learn_info()
cli_commands.purpose: Insight into ASIC NetFlow learn information — shows flow learning state and new-flow detection in the forwarding ASIC. Useful for diagnosing why new flows are not being created in hardware

cli_commands.command: show platform hardware fed switch active fwd-asic insight netflow_learn_info_flow_records()
cli_commands.purpose: Insight into ASIC NetFlow learned flow records — shows the actual flow records that have been learned by the forwarding ASIC. Compare against the software cache to verify hardware/software consistency

cli_commands.command: show platform hardware fed switch active fwd-asic insight netflow_records()
cli_commands.purpose: Insight into all ASIC NetFlow records — comprehensive dump of flow records stored in the forwarding ASIC hardware tables. This is the deepest hardware view of what the ASIC is tracking for NetFlow

cli_commands.command: show platform software process database fed switch active details LEABA_MAIN_DB "table la_fnf_records_handler_table" content
cli_commands.purpose: LEABA SDK internal FNF records handler table — shows the low-level Silicon One SDK records handler entries. Reveals how flow records are mapped to ASIC hardware handlers. Useful for deep debugging when records are not being processed correctly at the SDK level

cli_commands.command: show platform software process database fed switch active details LEABA_MAIN_DB "table la_fnf_mon_table" content
cli_commands.purpose: LEABA SDK internal FNF monitor table — shows the Silicon One SDK monitor entries including monitor-to-profile associations at the ASIC level. Verify that each configured monitor has a corresponding SDK entry

cli_commands.command: show platform software process database fed switch active details LEABA_MAIN_DB "table la_fnf_bind_table" content
cli_commands.purpose: LEABA SDK internal FNF bind table — shows the interface-to-monitor bindings at the Silicon One SDK level. Confirms which interfaces are bound to which monitors in the ASIC. Missing bindings mean the monitor is not programmed on that interface in hardware

cli_commands.command: show platform software process database fed switch active details LEABA_MAIN_DB "table la_fnf_feature_profile_table" content
cli_commands.purpose: LEABA SDK internal FNF feature profile table — shows the Silicon One SDK feature profile entries that define which packet fields the ASIC extracts for each FNF profile. Cross-reference with fnf profiles-dump to verify SDK-to-FED consistency

analysis_points: Record counts in ASIC (fnf record-count) should closely match the software cache entry count from 'show flow monitor cache'
Attach points must exist for every interface where the flow monitor is applied — missing attach points indicate a FED programming failure
TCAM table nfl_acl must have available entries for new monitors. If TCAM is exhausted, remove unused monitors or consolidate flow records
FED exporter-stats should track with IOS-level exporter stats. Discrepancies suggest internal FED-to-IOS communication issues
For stacked switches, repeat these commands with 'switch 1', 'switch 2', etc. to verify programming on each member
CRITICAL: If sw-stats-show reports errors, those errors indicate FED-level failures that may require a platform restart or TAC engagement
Always clear sw-stats (sw-stats-clear) before a repro attempt so that the delta counters reflect only the reproduced issue
Object-manager statistics (object-manager sw ac F0 statistics) should show zero failures — non-zero failures or growing pending counts indicate FED programming issues affecting FNF objects

Use fwd-asic insight netflow_* commands to get the deepest hardware view: netflow_debug_counters() for error counters, netflow_records() for ASIC-level flow entries, and netflow_config_info_profiles() for profile programming verification
ACL attachment-circuit app-type NETFLOW must show entries for each monitored interface — missing entries mean the NETFLOW ACL was not bound to the interface in the ASIC
Compare netflow_learn_info_flow_records() with 'show flow monitor cache' to verify hardware and software flow records are in sync — discrepancies indicate a FED-to-ASIC programming issue

LEABA process database tables (la_fnf_*) provide the deepest Silicon One SDK view: la_fnf_bind_table shows interface bindings, la_fnf_mon_table shows monitor entries, la_fnf_records_handler_table shows record handlers, and la_fnf_feature_profile_table shows field extraction profiles. Cross-reference these with FED-level dumps (monitors-dump, profiles-dump, attach-points-dump) to isolate FED-to-SDK programming failures

runbook_d29f2758.name: IPFIX Exporter Diagnostics
runbook_d29f2758.description: Focused diagnostic for IPFIX (IP Flow Information Export) specific issues. IPFIX is the IETF standard based on NetFlow v9 but uses different template mechanisms (Information Elements) and transport options. This sub-workflow covers IPFIX-specific statistics, template verification, and collector compatibility. Run 'NetFlow Common Baseline' parent workflow first.

cli_commands.purpose: Verify the export protocol is set to IPFIX (Version 10). Check transport port — IPFIX standard port is 4739 but many collectors use custom ports (2055, 9996, etc.)

interpretation_rules.message: Export protocol is not IPFIX — if IPFIX is required, reconfigure the exporter
interpretation_rules.recommendation: Under the flow exporter config, add 'export-protocol ipfix' to enable IPFIX export

cli_commands.purpose: IPFIX-specific export statistics including data records sent, template records sent, option records, and errors. Non-zero errors indicate IPFIX encoding or delivery issues
IPFIX template definitions — shows the Information Elements (IEs) included in each template. The collector must understand these IEs to decode the flow records
Generic template view — works for both NetFlow v9 and IPFIX. Shows template ID, field count, and refresh interval
Overall exporter statistics — cross-reference with IPFIX-specific stats to verify consistency

analysis_points: IPFIX uses 'export-protocol ipfix' under the exporter — the default is NetFlow v9. Verify this is set correctly
IPFIX template definitions must include all Information Elements that the collector expects. Mismatched IEs cause decode failures
IPFIX standard port is 4739 (TCP or UDP) but most deployments use UDP with a custom port (2055, 9996). Ensure the exporter port matches the collector configuration
Template refresh interval controls how often templates are re-sent. If the collector restarts, it needs a template before it can decode data — shorter refresh means faster recovery
IPFIX supports both UDP and TCP transport. TCP provides reliability but adds overhead. UDP is more common in practice

runbook_91de6a5d.name: ACL Access Control Common Baseline
runbook_91de6a5d.description: Baseline access-list troubleshooting for IPv4/IPv6 on Catalyst switches. Run these show commands first to list ACLs, where they are applied (Layer 2 port, SVI, VLAN map), and to confirm ACE order and hit counts. Configuration context and examples: Cisco IOS XE 17 ACLs Configuration Guide — Access Control Lists (Catalyst 9000): https://www.cisco.com/c/en/us/td/docs/switches/lan/c9000/security/acls/acls-configuration-guide/access-control-lists.html . Ingress precedence: Port ACL > VLAN map > Router ACL; egress: Router ACL > VLAN map > Port ACL. Sub-workflows: VLAN map / MAC ACL diagnostics, FED/ASIC drill-down: use sub-workflow that tracks runbooks/acl_debugging.docx.
symptoms: Traffic unexpectedly denied or permitted by ACL
After ACL change traffic still old behavior
Need to see where access lists are applied
Port ACL vs router ACL vs VLAN map confusion
IPv4 or IPv6 ACL on Catalyst switch not working

cli_commands.purpose: List all current IP and MAC access lists (numbered or named) with ACEs and, where supported, per-ACE match counters
IPv4: display all IP ACLs or use optional name/number to verify sequence order, remarks, and hit counts (L3 interface ACLs)
Drill into one IPv4 ACL by name or number — first-match wins; check implicit deny and logging ACEs if configured
List IPv6 access lists and ACEs; compare with where they are attached (ipv6 traffic-filter on interfaces)
IPv6 L3: confirm interface state and IPv6 traffic-filter / access-group style attachment for the path under test
View IPv4 named/numbered ACL definition blocks in the running config (including time-range references where used)
View IPv6 ACL configuration blocks in running-config
If ACLs use time-ranges, confirm active period vs current time (time-based entries may be inactive outside the range)
L3: confirm line-protocol/IP state and that ip access-group in/out is shown when an IPv4 ACL is applied
See interface config including ip access-group / ipv6 traffic-filter and L2 PACLs for the path under test
List MAC (Layer 2) access lists applied to L2 interfaces (optionally per-interface)

analysis_points: ACEs are processed in sequence order; first match wins. Implicit deny if no permit matches
Ingress (same VLAN/SVI path): port ACL, then VLAN map, then input router ACL; confirm which applies on the exact ingress
Egress: output router ACL, then VLAN map, then port ACL (see Cisco precedence for your software train)
On L2-only ports, router ACLs on the SVI do not filter host-to-host bridged traffic in the VLAN; use port ACL or VLAN map for bridged L2
Named ACLs allow per-line edits; resequence if rule order is wrong
If using time-ranges, verify the clock and time-range definition
For deeper VLAN map and MAC filter checks, use the VLAN map sub-workflow
For debug and hardware ACL usage, use the Debugging sub-workflow (may be CPU-impacting)

runbook_73d6d3cb.name: ACL VLAN Map and MAC Filter Diagnostics
runbook_73d6d3cb.description: VLAN access maps (VLAN maps) and MAC L2 filters. Use when security control is via vlan access-map / vlan filter or non-IP MAC ACLs. Run 'ACL Access Control Common Baseline' first. Maps match IP or MAC addresses against ACLs; action forward or drop applies to matched traffic.
symptoms: VLAN map not dropping or forwarding expected traffic
Bridged L2 traffic not hitting router ACL
MAC ACL on port not matching
Need to verify vlan filter to VLAN list mapping

cli_commands.purpose: Display all VLAN access maps, match clauses (ip/mac address), and action (forward/drop) per sequence
Show which VLAN map is applied to which VLAN IDs (vlan-list)
List MAC extended ACLs used in port ACLs or MAC VLAN map matches
Confirm MAC PACL on a specific L2 port (non-IP filtering)
Correlate VLAN map match ip/mac address ACLs with the same ACE view as the common baseline

analysis_points: A deny in a match ACL means 'no match' (not a drop) for that map line; a permit in the match ACL counts as a match, then use action
Only one VLAN map can be applied to a VLAN (check vlan filter mapping)
IP packets in VLAN maps: match ip address against standard/extended IP ACLs; non-IP uses MAC extended ACLs

Object-group (OG ACL) members when in use

Troubleshoot non-zero AOM before expecting clean ACL program

runbook_0995d6b0.name: CTS SGACL Policy, SGT Mapping, and Hardware Programming Verify
runbook_0995d6b0.description: Verify Cisco TrustSec SGACL enforcement on Catalyst/Argon devices: role-based ACL definition, IPv4/IPv6 or VLAN-to-SGT binding, SGT/DGT permission-cell binding, monitor-mode state, L2 CMD trust/propagation inputs, FED SGACL cell programming, Silicon One insight programming, object-manager health, counters, and SGACL logging. Built from SG-ACL and L2 CMD Argon TOI material.
symptoms: CTS SGACL not enforcing permit or deny action
SGACL deny traffic is still forwarded
SGACL monitor mode is expected but traffic is being dropped
role-based permissions from SGT to DGT missing
IP-SGT or VLAN-SGT mapping missing from hardware
SGT or DGT resolves to unknown zero unexpectedly
L2 CMD SGT is not trusted or not propagated
FED SGACL cell or ACL CG programming missing
object-manager pending acknowledgements for SGACL programming

cli_commands.purpose: Global CTS/SGACL configuration sweep. Confirm cts role-based enforced, SGT maps, permission bindings, monitor permissions, and logging interval are present in running-config.

interpretation_rules.message: Global CTS role-based enforcement is enabled.
Global CTS role-based enforcement is missing. SGACL cells may be programmed but not enforced.
interpretation_rules.recommendation: Configure global 'cts role-based enforced' unless this device is intentionally not enforcing SGACL.

interpretation_rules.message: CTS permission cell from {source_sgt} to {dest_sgt} is present in running-config.
CTS permission cell from {source_sgt} to {dest_sgt} is missing from running-config.
interpretation_rules.recommendation: Configure 'cts role-based permissions from {source_sgt} to {dest_sgt} {sgacl_name}' or the appropriate IPv4/IPv6/monitor variant.

cli_commands.purpose: Interface CTS/L2 CMD check. Confirm cts manual, trust/default SGT, propagate/no propagate, device-tracking attach-policy, and per-interface enforcement disable if present.

interpretation_rules.message: {interface} has 'cts manual' configured.
{interface} trusts incoming L2 CMD carried SGT or has a trusted default SGT.
{interface} has 'no propagate sgt'. Egress L2 CMD SGT propagation is intentionally disabled.
interpretation_rules.recommendation: If downstream devices expect L2 CMD SGT propagation, remove 'no propagate sgt' under the interface CTS manual context.

interpretation_rules.message: {interface} disables CTS role-based enforcement locally.
interpretation_rules.recommendation: Remove interface-level 'no cts role-based enforced' if SGACL must enforce on this egress interface.

cli_commands.purpose: Verify the role-based ACL exists and contains the expected permit/deny/log ACEs.

interpretation_rules.message: Role-based ACL {sgacl_name} exists.
Role-based ACL {sgacl_name} is missing or is not shown as a role-based ACL.
interpretation_rules.recommendation: Create it with 'ip access-list role-based {sgacl_name}' and add the expected ACEs.

interpretation_rules.message: At least one ACE in {sgacl_name} uses the log keyword.

cli_commands.purpose: Verify active IPv4 and IPv6 IP-SGT bindings. The TOI shows both IPv4 and IPv6 bindings and active binding summaries as the IOS-level source of SGT mapping truth.

interpretation_rules.message: Source IP {source_ip} appears in active CTS IP-SGT bindings.
Source IP {source_ip} is missing from active CTS IP-SGT bindings. Source SGT may resolve to unknown/0 unless learned through trusted L2 CMD or another source.
interpretation_rules.recommendation: Configure or fix the source SGT mapping, for example 'cts role-based sgt-map {source_ip} sgt {source_sgt}', or verify VLAN/device tracking based SGT mapping.

interpretation_rules.message: Expected source SGT {source_sgt} appears in active CTS bindings.

cli_commands.purpose: Verify SGACL permission matrix cells, configured ACL names, and whether a cell is in monitor mode.

interpretation_rules.field: permission cell from source to destination present
interpretation_rules.message: Permission cell {source_sgt}->{dest_sgt} exists in show cts role-based permissions.
Permission output references SGACL {sgacl_name}.

interpretation_rules.field: permission cell missing in IOS view
interpretation_rules.message: Permission cell {source_sgt}->{dest_sgt} is missing in the IOS CTS permission matrix.
interpretation_rules.recommendation: Configure 'cts role-based permissions from {source_sgt} to {dest_sgt} {sgacl_name}' or the protocol-specific/monitor variant.

interpretation_rules.message: At least one configured permission cell is in monitor mode. Monitor mode counts permits/denies but does not drop packets.

cli_commands.command: show cts role-based permissions from {source_sgt} to {dest_sgt}
cli_commands.purpose: Focused view of the expected SGT/DGT permission cell. This should show the bound SGACL name and monitor-mode state for the target cell.

interpretation_rules.message: Focused cell {source_sgt}->{dest_sgt} references SGACL {sgacl_name}.
Focused cell {source_sgt}->{dest_sgt} does not reference SGACL {sgacl_name}.
interpretation_rules.recommendation: Verify source/destination SGT values and bind the expected SGACL to the cell.

interpretation_rules.pattern: RBACL Monitor All for Configured Policies : FALSE
interpretation_rules.message: Configured policies are not globally monitor-only for this output.

cli_commands.purpose: Check software/hardware permit, deny, and monitor counters for the target cell. For monitor-mode policy, packets are counted under monitor but not dropped.

interpretation_rules.message: Counter row exists for SGACL cell {source_sgt}->{dest_sgt}.
No counter row found for SGACL cell {source_sgt}->{dest_sgt}. The cell may not be programmed or traffic is not matching this SGT/DGT pair.
interpretation_rules.recommendation: Verify SGT/DGT resolution, permission binding, and hardware cell programming.

cli_commands.command: show platform software fed active acl sgacl cell all
cli_commands.purpose: FED SGACL cell summary. Confirms SGT/DGT cell, monitor-mode bit, counter OID, and ACL CG ID are present in FED.

interpretation_rules.message: FED SGACL cell {source_sgt}->{dest_sgt} is present in cell summary.
FED SGACL cell {source_sgt}->{dest_sgt} is missing. IOS permission may not have programmed into FED.
interpretation_rules.recommendation: Check object-manager pending ACKs and FED SGACL traces; verify the permission cell and SGACL ACL CG are valid.

cli_commands.command: show platform software fed active acl sgacl cell {source_sgt} {dest_sgt}
cli_commands.purpose: Per-cell FED SGACL details. The TOI notes this command shows all ASIC details for the individual cell, including IPv4/IPv6 protocol entries, cell OIDs, counter OIDs, and ACL CG IDs.

interpretation_rules.message: FED has an IPv4 SGACL cell entry for {source_sgt}->{dest_sgt}.
FED has an IPv6 SGACL cell entry for {source_sgt}->{dest_sgt}.
FED per-cell output includes counter OIDs.
FED per-cell output includes ACL CG_ID.

cli_commands.command: show platform hardware fed switch active fwd-asic insight s1_sgt_mapping_status_v4(sec_grp_map_prefix='{source_ip_prefix}')
cli_commands.purpose: Silicon One IPv4 SGT mapping verification for the source prefix. The TOI recommends passing filter arguments and no spaces between insight arguments.

interpretation_rules.message: Silicon One insight shows source prefix {source_ip_prefix} in IPv4 SGT mapping.
Silicon One insight output includes expected source SGT {source_sgt}.
Silicon One IPv4 SGT mapping is missing source prefix {source_ip_prefix}.
interpretation_rules.recommendation: Check IOS IP-SGT binding, l3_fib/FED programming path, and object-manager pending ACKs.

cli_commands.command: show platform hardware fed switch active fwd-asic insight s1_sec_groups_matrix_map_status(sgt={source_sgt},dgt={dest_sgt},ip_version='IPV4')
cli_commands.purpose: Silicon One SGACL matrix map verification for the target source/destination SGT pair.

interpretation_rules.message: Silicon One matrix output includes source SGT {source_sgt}.
Silicon One matrix output includes destination SGT {dest_sgt}.

interpretation_rules.field: S1 matrix empty or missing cell
interpretation_rules.message: Silicon One matrix output appears empty or does not contain the SGACL cell.
interpretation_rules.recommendation: Verify FED SGACL cell output and object-manager state; collect s1sdk-api/ra/hld/lld traces if programming is missing.

cli_commands.command: show platform software process database fed active details LEABA_MAIN_DB "table la_sgacl_cell_table" content
cli_commands.purpose: FED internal TDL database dump for SGACL cell table. Use to confirm la_sgacl_cell_table has the programmed cell state.

interpretation_rules.message: FED LEABA_MAIN_DB la_sgacl_cell_table dump was returned.

cli_commands.command: show platform software process database fed active details LEABA_MAIN_DB "table la_acl_cg_table" content
cli_commands.purpose: FED internal TDL database dump for ACL CG table. Use to confirm ACL content group programming associated with SGACL policies.

interpretation_rules.message: FED LEABA_MAIN_DB la_acl_cg_table dump was returned.

interpretation_rules.field: SGACL ACL name in CG table
interpretation_rules.message: ACL CG table references SGACL name {sgacl_name}.

cli_commands.command: show platform software fed switch active acl info db details
cli_commands.purpose: ACL details from the NPD table. Use this when ACL exists in IOS but hardware/FED programming looks wrong.

interpretation_rules.message: FED ACL DB details were returned.

cli_commands.command: show platform software fed switch active acl info sdk details
cli_commands.purpose: ACL details from the NPL/SDK table. Use to cross-check SDK programming against DB details.

interpretation_rules.message: FED ACL SDK details were returned.

cli_commands.command: show platform software object-manager switch active F0 statistics
cli_commands.purpose: Forwarding-manager object-manager health. Pending acknowledgements, error objects, or stale objects can explain missing SGACL/SGT hardware programming.

interpretation_rules.message: Object-manager reports one or more error objects. Hardware programming may have failed.
interpretation_rules.recommendation: Inspect pending-ack-update and collect forwarding-manager/fed SGACL traces.

interpretation_rules.message: Object-manager has pending issues. SGACL programming may still be in progress or stuck.
interpretation_rules.recommendation: Check pending-ack-update and repeat after a short interval.

interpretation_rules.message: Object-manager has pending acknowledgements. FED/ASIC programming may not be complete.
interpretation_rules.recommendation: Check pending-ack-update for stuck objects and collect SDK traces if it does not drain.

cli_commands.command: show platform software object-manager switch active F0 pending-ack-update
cli_commands.purpose: List pending object-manager updates if SGACL, ACL CG, SGT mapping, or matrix objects are stuck waiting for acknowledgement.

interpretation_rules.field: pending ACK output has SGACL/ACL objects
interpretation_rules.message: Pending-ack-update includes ACL/SGACL related objects.
interpretation_rules.recommendation: Correlate object IDs with FED SGACL cell/ACL CG table and collect forwarding-manager/FED traces.

cli_commands.purpose: SGACL and ACL logging verification. SGACL log messages use %RBM-6-SGACLHIT; traditional ACL log messages use %SEC-6-IPACCESSLOGDP.

interpretation_rules.message: SGACL hit logging is present in syslog.
Traditional ACL dataplane logging is present in syslog.
No SGACLHIT or IPACCESSLOGDP messages found in current logging buffer.
interpretation_rules.recommendation: Verify ACEs use 'log', configure 'cts role-based enforcement logging-interval <5-86400>' as needed, clear logging, generate test traffic, then re-check logs.

cli_commands.command: show platform software fed switch active punt ios-cause brief
cli_commands.purpose: Punt ios-cause summary from the SGACL logging debug section. Use when SGACL logging/mirroring path is suspected.

cli_commands.command: show platform software fed switch active punt asic-cause brief
cli_commands.purpose: Punt asic-cause summary from the SGACL logging debug section. Use when SGACL logging/mirroring path is suspected.

analysis_points: This runbook targets real CTS/SGACL policy and SGT programming, not FNF SGT flow-monitor verification.

Primary pass condition: source and destination tags resolve correctly, the {source_sgt}->{dest_sgt} permission cell references {sgacl_name}, the cell appears in FED and S1 insight, object-manager has no SGACL-related errors/pending ACKs, and counters/logs match the generated traffic.

CTS enforcement from the TOI: SGT lookup, matrix lookup, ACL TCAM match, and action happen in the SGACL path. SGACL enforcement is intended for the last egress stage. Multicast and broadcast traffic are not subject to SGACL enforcement, and enforcement on virtual interfaces such as SVI is not supported.

SGT priority from the TOI: highest priority is trusted L2 CMD carried SGT from a trusted interface and VXLAN-carried SGT; next are IP-based and VLAN-based SGT; then device SGT; then untrusted-port static SGT.

Role-based ACL sample from screenshot: 'ip access-list role-based acl1', then ACEs such as 'permit tcp' and 'permit udp'. Verification: 'show ip access-lists acl1' should display 'Role-based IP access list acl1'.

IP-SGT map sample from screenshot: 'cts role-based sgt-map 100.1.1.1 sgt 1001' and 'cts role-based sgt-map 100::1 sgt 1001'. Verification: 'show cts role-based sgt-map all' should show active IPv4-SGT and IPv6-SGT binding summaries.

VLAN-to-SGT mapping from slide: configure device tracking policy, attach it to the access interface, and configure 'cts role-based sgt-map vlan-list <vlan-id> sgt <sgt-number>'. Device tracking is required for VLAN-based SGT mapping.

Permission-cell bind sample from screenshot: 'cts role-based permissions from 100 to 101 cts_dall'. Verification: 'show cts role-based permissions from 100 to 101' should show IPv4 role-based permissions from group 100 to group 101 with SGACL cts_dall.
Monitor-mode bind sample from screenshot: 'cts role-based monitor permissions from 100 to 102'. In monitor mode permit/deny counters update, but packets are not dropped.
L2 CMD propagation from screenshot: under interface 'cts manual', 'no propagate sgt' explicitly disables egress propagation. Propagation is otherwise enabled by default when CTS manual is configured.
L2 CMD ingress trust from screenshot: under interface 'cts manual', 'policy static sgt 1000 trusted' configures the interface to trust incoming L2 CMD SGT or use the configured default SGT.

Hardware verification from TOI: use 'show platform software fed active acl sgacl cell all' for summary; use 'show platform software fed active acl sgacl cell <sgt> <dgt>' for all ASIC details of an individual cell.

Silicon One insight verification from TOI: use 'show platform hardware fed switch active fwd-asic insight s1_sgt_mapping_status_v4(sec_grp_map_prefix='<prefix>')' for filtered IPv4 SGT mapping. Do not insert spaces between insight arguments; quote string arguments.
Silicon One matrix verification from TOI: use s1_sec_groups_matrix_map_status with sgt, dgt, and ip_version to confirm S1 SDK matrix-map programming for the cell.
FED internal DB verification from TOI: la_acl_cg_table tracks ACL content group data; la_acl_bind_table, la_sgacl_intf_table, and la_sgacl_cell_table expose SGACL-related programmed state.

Object-manager verification from screenshot: 'show platform software object-manager switch active F0 statistics' should not show error objects, stale objects, or stuck pending acknowledgements for SGACL programming.

Logging from TOI: ACEs need 'log'; SGACL logging interval is configured by 'cts role-based enforcement logging-interval <5-86400>'. SGACL logs appear as %RBM-6-SGACLHIT. Traditional ACL logs appear as %SEC-6-IPACCESSLOGDP.
